# Snap packs | Lob Help Center

A snap pack is a pressure-sealed mailer with perforated sides for easy opening. Snap pack mailers are versatile and can be used by a wide range of industries including Financial Services, Education, and Healthcare. The official-looking nature of snap packs demands attention, offers unique tactile engagement, and has one of the highest open rates among mail types.

To get started with snap packs, check out our [API documentationarrow-up-right](https://docs.lob.com/#tag/Snap-Packs)
, [arrow-up-right](https://github.com/lob/examples/)
or [Template Galleryarrow-up-right](https://www.lob.com/template-gallery#letters)
 for inspiration.

circle-info

Snap packs are exclusive to Enterprise edition customers. Upgrade your [Print & Mail editionarrow-up-right](https://dashboard.lob.com/#/settings/editions)
 to gain access to this mail format, or reach out to our [sales teamarrow-up-right](https://www.lob.com/sales)
 to learn more.

circle-check

Snap packs currently have a 4-day SLA.

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/snap-packs#layout-dimensions-and-specs)

Layout dimensions & specs


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/snap-packs#layout-dimensions)

Layout dimensions

Lob’s snap pack offering has a flat, final trim size of 8.5x11”, a folded (in half) dimension of 8.5x5.5", and three perforated sides. Snaps packs are printed double-sided in black and white or color.

Reference our design templates on where to place your design elements; carefully note the location of the address block and other ink-free zones.

*   [8.5x11” snap pack templatearrow-up-right](https://s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/public.lob.com/assets/8.5x11_Snappack_template_address.pdf)
    

Snap packs DO NOT need additional trim space added to their dimensions because their production journey does not involve trimming (the way a postcard would). Please set your document sizing to the above dimensions when submitting to Lob.

circle-exclamation

**Make sure all artwork submissions are facing upright for both inside and outside panels**. Our printers will invert the inside panel of submitted customer artwork during the production phase.

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/snap-packs#paper-specs)

Paper specs

We use a few pre-approved papers across our commercial printer network. In ensuring uniform quality and consistency across all of our mail pieces, each paper source specification must fall within a small range.

The specification/value range pairs are:

*   8.5 x 11" B&W & color snap packs
    
*   Basis Weight: 80# text
    
*   Bi-fold
    

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/snap-packs#ink-free-zone-dimensions)

Ink-free zone dimensions

8.5x11" snap packs:

*   ½” around the entire flat piece (for perforation)
    

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/snap-packs#design-best-practices-10)

Snap pack best practices


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Ensure your snap pack self-mailers get to their final destination by following these guidelines:

*   The `from` field is required for all self-mailers, regardless of the destination
    
*   Snap pack should not contain any PII outside of the [no-ink zonearrow-up-right](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/artboard-layout#address-block-postage-8)
    
*   Avoid any [prohibited artworkarrow-up-right](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/artboard-layout#prohibited-artwork-11)
     to ensure your mail does not get rejected by USPS
    
*   HIPAA-compliant snap packs are offered at this time, so long as all HIPAA related information is on the inside of the snap pack.
    
*   [Mailing Classarrow-up-right](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/mailing-class-and-postage#available-mailing-class-options-5)
     options may differ based on destination and use type (e.g. promotional vs non-promotional)
    
*   We do not recommend placing promotional address information in your creative in the bottom 2.375" of a mailpiece _opposite the address panel_. This will prevent the USPS from accidentally scanning the wrong side of the mailpiece. [Learn more here.](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/artboard-layout#address-zone-warning)

# Certified Mail or Registered Mail | Lob Help Center

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/mailing-classes-and-postage/certified-mail-or-registered-mail#certified-mail-electronic-return-receipts-5)

Certified Mail

Certified Mail is an add-on service offered by USPS for First Class mail that provides proof of mailing to the sender. With electronic USPS [tracking](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/getting-data-and-results/tracking-your-mail#tracking-information-for-registered-certified-mail-14)
, the sender is notified when the mailing was delivered or that a delivery attempt was made. An electronic return receipt showing the recipient's signature is also available for Certified Mail only. Both the Certified Mail and the electronic return receipt add-ons are only available for First Class mail sent domestically within the US.

You send letters as Certified Mail through the Lob API by passing the value `certified` in the `extra_services` parameter of the [create letter requestarrow-up-right](https://docs.lob.com/#operation/letter_create)
. You may similarly opt for an electronic return receipt by passing `certified_return_receipt` in the `extra_services` parameter in the [APIarrow-up-right](https://docs.lob.com/#operation/letter_create)
.

Tracking events are viewable directly within the Lob dashboard or via the API, similar to how they are for non-Certified letters, and you can also subscribe to webhook events for Certified letters. When you make a request for Certified Mail you will immediately receive a carrier tracking number (retrievable via your Lob dashboard), which can be used to track the mail via the carrier’s website - or you can choose to track the mail via the scan events within your Lob dashboard. The resulting screen should appear in your dashboard:

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FQomFRb6j5FWeFyds23oV%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D5d0f16b4-a5ba-48fa-a3d3-98caa426a42a&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=6ab30d2f&sv=2)

circle-info

Tracking events for Certified Mail are **different** from tracking events received for regular mail types; see the full list in our [API documentation for Certified tracking event detailsarrow-up-right](https://docs.lob.com/#tag/Tracking-Events)
.

Visit the [Certified Mail envelopes section](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/letter-envelopes#certified-mail-envelopes-1)
 for more guidance around sending Certified Mail.

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/mailing-classes-and-postage/certified-mail-or-registered-mail#electronic-return-receipts)

Electronic Return Receipts

An Electronic Return Receipt is available to the sender as a USPS [add-on](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/ready-to-get-started/pricing-details#letters-6)
 to Certified Mail. This receipt will allow you to download a digital copy of the recipient's signature used to sign for the delivery of the letter. You opt for an Electronic Return Receipt by passing `certified_return_receipt` in the `extra_service` parameter of [creating a letterarrow-up-right](https://docs.lob.com/#tag/Letters/operation/letter_create)
.

The return receipt will be accessible via the certified tracking number from the [USPS homepagearrow-up-right](https://www.usps.com/)
. However, if you would like to receive the signature PDF via email, follow these steps:

*   Visit [www.usps.comarrow-up-right](http://www.usps.com/)
    
*   Enter the Certified tracking number into the Track a Package box
    
*   Click the "Return Receipt Email" dropdown and fill the required fields in the form
    

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FIT3cNArAtDQeGwuY4ytW%252Fexpected_delivery.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D2dfab7a6-45c6-42ab-9c7e-906a372fcd89&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=934500a3&sv=2)

*   The USPS will send you an email with the signature file like the below example
    

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252Fok9tayGKPqp8GxSa4sQ7%252FPOD.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D3a788df6-42d9-4b44-8990-d3e2c659fb4b&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=7c5d794b&sv=2)

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/mailing-classes-and-postage/certified-mail-or-registered-mail#registered-mail-6)

Registered Mail

circle-check

The USPS Registered Mail add-on is only available for First Class mail and can only be sent (and tracked) domestically.

Registered Mail is an add-on service offered by USPS that provides extra protection for high-value letters and packages. When you send a letter by Registered Mail, the USPS establishes a chain of custody that [tracks](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/getting-data-and-results/tracking-your-mail#tracking-information-for-registered-certified-mail-14)
 and secures your shipment throughout the entire transit process—from the moment it is dropped off at the Post Office until the moment it’s delivered.

You can send letters as Registered Mail through the Lob API by passing the value `registered` in the `extra_services` parameter of the [create letter requestarrow-up-right](https://docs.lob.com/#operation/letter_create)
.

Letters sent as `registered` will receive a different set of scan events compared to regular First or Standard Class mail. Registered Mail will instead receive a carrier tracking number and link, which is an add-on that will be available 3 business days following the mailer’s `send_date`. This tracking number can be used to track the mailer via the carrier’s website.

[PreviousMailing Classes & Postagechevron-left](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/mailing-classes-and-postage)
[NextInternational mailchevron-right](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/international-mail)

Last updated 6 months ago

Was this helpful?

*   [Electronic Return Receipts](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/mailing-classes-and-postage/certified-mail-or-registered-mail#electronic-return-receipts)

# USPS Promotions Through Lob | Lob Help Center

At Lob, we’re committed to helping you take advantage of every opportunity to save on postage costs. Each year, the USPS offers various promotions that provide discounts on mailing services, and we’re here to support you through the process of applying for and meeting the requirements for these offers. In this section, you’ll find detailed guides that walk you through each USPS promotion, helping you understand how to qualify, how to apply, and how our platform can seamlessly support you in the process. Whether you’re a seasoned mailer or new to USPS promotions, we’ve got you covered with all the information you need to maximize your savings.

Ready to start saving? Explore the guidebooks below to get started!

Past acceptance/approval in prior year promotion does not guarantee acceptance/approval in current year promotions. Refer to each promotion for enrollment and acceptance criteria.

# Postcards | Lob Help Center

Postcards stand out in direct marketing for their ability to deliver eye-catching, concise content that engages recipients instantly, making them highly effective for promotional campaigns. For transactional or operational communications, their streamlined, envelope-free design ensures important information is immediately visible, enhancing clarity and response rates for critical updates or calls to action.

Let's get started! First, be sure to check out the following resources:

*   API documentation: [Postcards APIarrow-up-right](https://docs.lob.com/#tag/Postcards)
     or [Campaigns APIarrow-up-right](https://docs.lob.com/#tag/Campaigns)
    
*   Lob dashboard: [Postcardsarrow-up-right](https://dashboard.lob.com/postcards)
     section or [Campaignsarrow-up-right](https://dashboard.lob.com/campaigns/)
     for postcard campaigns
    
*   [GitHub libraryarrow-up-right](https://github.com/lob/examples/)
     for HTML templates
    
*   [Template Galleryarrow-up-right](https://www.lob.com/template-gallery#postcards)
     for ready-to-use creatives and design inspiration
    

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/postcards#postcard-back-2)

Layout dimensions & specs


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/postcards#layout-dimensions)

Layout dimensions

The front side of the postcard is fully available for customization.

For the backside of the postcard, follow the guidance provided in the templates below to leave enough room for ink-free areas.

*   [4x6" postcard templatearrow-up-right](https://s3-us-west-2.amazonaws.com/public.lob.com/assets/templates/postcards/4x6_postcard.pdf)
    
*   [5x7" postcard templatearrow-up-right](https://s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/public.lob.com/assets/templates/postcards/5x7_postcard.pdf)
    
*   [6x9" postcard templatearrow-up-right](https://s3-us-west-2.amazonaws.com/public.lob.com/assets/templates/postcards/6x9_postcard.pdf)
    
*   [6x11" postcard templatearrow-up-right](https://s3-us-west-2.amazonaws.com/public.lob.com/assets/templates/postcards/6x11_postcard.pdf)
    

You may also [add QR codes](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/maximizing-engagement/adding-qr-codes)
 to your postcards to incorporate personalized tracking.

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/postcards#paper-specs-3)

Paper specs

We use a few pre-approved papers across our commercial printer network. In ensuring uniform quality and consistency across all of our mail pieces; each paper source specification must fall within a small range. The specification/value range pairs are listed below:

*   Basis Weight: 100# - 120#
    
*   Cover GSM: 255 - 325
    
*   Full Bleed
    
*   1 Side UV Gloss
    

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/postcards#ink-free-zone-dimensions-4)

Ink-free zone dimensions

4x6" Postcards:

*   W x H: 3.2835 x 2.375"
    
*   0.275" from the right edge (including bleed)
    
*   0.25" from the bottom edge (including bleed)
    

All other postcard sizes:

*   W x H: 4 x 2.375"
    
*   0.275" from the right edge (including bleed)
    
*   0.25" from the bottom edge (including bleed)
    

circle-check

For all postcards printed by Lob, a Carbon Neutral Mail certification will automatically be included within the official use address block on the backside. Read more about how you can send mail in a more environmentally responsible way with Lob in our [Carbon Neutral Mail](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/artboard-layout#carbon-neutral-mail-certification-9)
 section.

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/postcards#mailing-postcards-5)

Postcard best practices


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Ensure your postcards get to their final destination by following these guidelines:

*   All US postcards can be sent as either First Class or Marketing Mail (Standard Class Mail)
    
*   The `from` field is not required for US postcards, but is required for international destinations
    
*   Only 4x6" postcards can be sent to [international destinations](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/international-mail)
    
*   Postcards cannot include any PII outside of the [no-ink zone](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/artboard-layout#address-block-postage-8)
    
*   Avoid any [prohibited artwork](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/artboard-layout#prohibited-artwork-11)
     to ensure your mail does not get rejected by USPS
    
*   We do not recommend placing promotional address information in your creative in the bottom 2.375" of a mailpiece _opposite the address panel_. This will prevent the USPS from accidentally scanning the wrong side of the mailpiece. [Learn more here.](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/artboard-layout#address-zone-warning)
    

[PreviousMail piece design specschevron-left](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs)
[NextSelf-Mailerschevron-right](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/self-mailers)

# Using metadata | Lob Help Center

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/managing-mail-settings/using-metadata#metadata-tags)

Metadata tags


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In the Lob ecosystem, metadata is used to “tag” each mail piece or each campaign. You can use metadata to find individual mail pieces more easily and group mail pieces together for reporting and attribution. Example metadata tags could include a campaign name, a customer segment, or an internal ID. Using metadata smartly can help bridge the gap between Lob and your internal systems, allow you track mail at a more granular level, and create data-driven stories to get the most ROI out of the direct mail channel.

See [here for more coding examples using metadata tagsarrow-up-right](https://help.lob.com/developer-docs/use-case-guides/mass-deletion-setup#use-lob-metadata)
.

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/managing-mail-settings/using-metadata#metadata-types)

Metadata types

There are several kinds of metadata at Lob:

*   **Mailpiece metadata** can be used on our single mailpiece APIs.
    
*   **Campaign metadata** can be used with our Campaigns feature, and allow you to filter your searches by all mailpieces in a particular campaign.
    
*   **Recipient metadata** can be used within our Campaign UI feature in the dashboard, and allow you to filter by a specific subset of mailpieces.
    

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/managing-mail-settings/using-metadata#key-value-pairs)

Key-value pairs

Both mailpiece and campaign metadata rely on **key-value pairs** that you create.

*   The **key**, or **tag name**, is what will remain consistent across all the mailpieces you’d like to create. For example, “state”.
    
*   The **value**, or **tag value**, will be unique to each mailpiece, for example “NC” or “CA”.
    
*   When combined, you can easily find mailpieces with “`state:NC`” or “`state:CA`”.
    

Metadata objects can include up to 20 key-value pairs of custom data. Each metadata key must be less than 40 characters long and values must be less than 500 characters. Neither can contain the characters `"` and `\`. Metadata does not support nested objects.

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/managing-mail-settings/using-metadata#mailpiece-metadata-16)

Mailpiece metadata


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Mailpiece metadata applies to each individual mailpiece sent. Mailpiece metadata is great for tags that are specific to each recipient, like `state:NC` or `first_name:John`.

You can create mailpiece metadata on our single mailpiece APIs by including a metadata object with each mailpiece `POST` request. Here is an example of a mailpiece `POST` request to our `api.lob.com/v1/postcards` endpoint, including a metadata object:

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/managing-mail-settings/using-metadata#campaign-metadata-17)

Campaign metadata


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Metadata at the campaign level is configured on the `POST /v1/campaigns` [API callarrow-up-right](https://docs.lob.com/#tag/Campaigns)
 or in the dashboard, in the Campaigns [Configure Settingsarrow-up-right](https://dashboard.lob.com/campaigns/create/settings)
 screen at the bottom “Add Tags” section.

circle-exclamation

Campaign metadata **cannot be configured within your campaign CSV**

Each individual mailpiece created through the campaign will inherit Campaign metadata. This is great for tags you’d like to apply to an entire campaign.

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/managing-mail-settings/using-metadata#recipient-metadata)

Recipient metadata

In addition to Campaign metadata, you can assign metadata on the individual recipient level. This is great for instances in which you'd like to apply context specific to each individual such as a unique identifier, customer id, or email address.

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/managing-mail-settings/using-metadata#filtering-your-metadata-18)

Filtering your metadata


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In the dashboard, you can filter metadata at the single mailpiece level. Any mailpiece created in the Campaigns function will also be visible within the single mail piece tabs.

Example: In the [Postcardsarrow-up-right](https://dashboard.lob.com/postcards)
 section, click on ‘Filters’ and search by Metadata

​![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fstatic.helpjuice.com%2Fhelpjuice_production%2Fuploads%2Fupload%2Fimage%2F9583%2Fdirect%2F1656632218598-1656632218598.png&width=300&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=4893bf7d&sv=2)

You can also go into any individual mailpiece, and see the key-value pairs tagged in the 'Metadata' section under the 'Details' section.

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fstatic.helpjuice.com%2Fhelpjuice_production%2Fuploads%2Fupload%2Fimage%2F9583%2Fdirect%2F1629279221778-1629279221778.png&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=bd707c71&sv=2)

# First Class Mail Advertising | Lob Help Center

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/usps-promotions-through-lob/first-class-mail-advertising#what-is-the-first-class-mail-a-dvertising-promotion)

What is the First-Class Mail Advertising promotion?

The First-Class Mail Advertising promotion offers businesses a unique opportunity to blend the reliability and prestige of First-Class Mail with impactful advertising. By leveraging First-Class Mail’s superior delivery service and incorporating effective marketing strategies, you can unlock powerful ways to connect with your audience and achieve your marketing goals.

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/usps-promotions-through-lob/first-class-mail-advertising#when-is-this-promotion-live)

When is this promotion live?

The First-Class Marketing Mail promotion runs from **September 1, 2026, to December 31, 2026**.

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/usps-promotions-through-lob/first-class-mail-advertising#how-can-i-take-advantage-of-the-first-class-a-dvertising-mail-promotion-with-lob)

How can I take advantage of the First-Class Advertising Mail promotion with Lob?

The easiest way to take advantage of this promotion with Lob?

*   **Add a marketing message**: Update your operational or transactional mailpieces to include at least one marketing message directly on the mailpiece.
    
    *   Discount offer example: “Save 10% on your next purchase with this exclusive coupon!”
        
    *   Limited-time offer example: “Act now! This offer expires in 7 days.”
        
    
*   **Upgrade to First-Class Mail**: Ensure your mailpieces are sent using First-Class Mail to qualify for the discount.
    
*   **First-Class Mail, including letters, cards, and flats**, is eligible for this promotion.
    
*   Marketing messages must be on the mailpiece itself. Those that only appear on buck slips, stickers, etc. will be disqualified.
    
*   Content that promotes mail diversion (i.e. “Go paperless” or “Switch to e-billing” will disqualify your mailpieces.
    

Here’s everything you need to know to qualify for a **5% discount**.

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/usps-promotions-through-lob/first-class-mail-advertising#opt-in-to-the-promotion)

Opt-in to the promotion

To get started, log into the Lob Promotions Portal: [https://dashboard.lob.com/promotions/available-promotionsarrow-up-right](https://dashboard.lob.com/promotions/available-promotions)

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/usps-promotions-through-lob/first-class-mail-advertising#id-1.-select-the-available-promotion)

# USPS Secure Destruction | Lob Help Center

Secure Destruction is a free service provided by the United States Postal Service (USPS) that offers a secure method for handling undeliverable First-Class mail. This service is especially beneficial for customers managing sensitive documents, such as those containing Personal Identifiable Information (PII), financial data, or medical information, while also helping reduce costs and environmental impact.

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/send-mail/usps-secure-destruction#what-is-secure-destruction)

What is Secure Destruction?

Secure Destruction ensures that any undeliverable First-Class mail is securely destroyed at USPS facilities. Rather than being returned to the sender, mail is processed and eliminated under strict security protocols, preventing any potential unauthorized access.

Lob provides this service at no cost to customers who send First-Class mail, and it can be easily integrated into existing workflows for organizations that regularly deal with undeliverable mail.

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/send-mail/usps-secure-destruction#why-use-secure-destruction)

Why Use Secure Destruction?

1.  Enhanced Customer Security Secure Destruction offers a significant value-add for organizations that handle sensitive customer data, such as PII, financial records, or medical information. By ensuring that undeliverable mail is destroyed securely, companies can maintain compliance with data protection regulations, reduce the risk of breaches, and improve customer trust.
    
2.  Cost Savings Secure Destruction helps customers avoid the financial burden of handling undeliverable mail. For example, companies that do not use this service may need to contract with third-party vendors or allocate in-house resources to manage the destruction of returned mail.
    
3.  Reduce Environmental Footprint Secure Destruction also offers environmental benefits by eliminating the reverse logistics of returning mailpieces to senders. By preventing the need for transportation and manual disposal, carbon emissions are reduced. This aligns with Lob's sustainability mission to minimize the environmental impact of mail processing.
    

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/send-mail/usps-secure-destruction#how-secure-destruction-works)

How Secure Destruction Works

Secure Destruction is available exclusively for First-Class letters and snap packs. If your organization sends these types of mail, you can easily enable Secure Destruction through your account page. Once activated, any undeliverable First-Class mail will be securely destroyed at USPS facilities, ensuring your sensitive documents are handled with the highest level of security.

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/send-mail/usps-secure-destruction#who-benefits-from-secure-destruction)

Who Benefits from Secure Destruction?

Secure Destruction is ideal for First-Class mailers, particularly those who manage sensitive information or face significant logistical challenges in processing undeliverable mail. This includes:

*   Financial institutions
    
*   Health care providers
    
*   Insurance providers
    
*   Public and private education institutions
    
*   Government agencies
    
*   Utility companies
    
*   Mail service providers
    
*   Legal service providers
    

Once your organization opts into Secure Destruction, Lob will track your undeliverable mailpieces via USPS scanning events. You will be able to confirm that the mail has been destroyed through these tracking events, offering you peace of mind that your sensitive documents have been successfully processed by USPS.

[PreviousSend campaigns via the Campaigns APIchevron-left](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/send-mail/send-campaigns-via-the-campaigns-api)
[NextGetting data & resultschevron-right](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/getting-data-and-results)

Last updated 1 year ago

# Letters | Lob Help Center

Letters are highly effective for direct marketing due to their personal touch and tangible presence, enhancing recipient engagement. They also serve well for transactional or operational communications, offering a formal and reliable delivery of important information that stands out from digital clutter.

Lob offers two sizes of letters: 8.5x11" and 8.5x14".

To get started with sending letters, check out our [Letters API documentationarrow-up-right](https://docs.lob.com/#tag/Letters)
, our [GitHub libraryarrow-up-right](https://github.com/lob/examples/)
, or Lob's [Template Galleryarrow-up-right](https://www.lob.com/template-gallery#letters)
 for inspiration.

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/letters#dimensions-specs-1)

Layout dimensions & specs


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/letters#layout-dimensions)

**Layout dimensions**

**8.5x11" letters**

Lob offers `8.5" x 11"` letters in both black & white and in color, which can be printed single or double-sided. Letters and checks not exceeding 6 tri-folded sheets of paper in total will be sent in standard [#10 double-windowed outer envelopes](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/letter-envelopes#standard-10-double-windowed-outer-envelope)
. Letters and checks over 6 sheets of paper but not exceeding 60 total sheets of paper will be mailed in a [flat envelope](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/letter-envelopes#9-x-12-single-window-flat-outer-envelope)
.

Reference our design templates on where to place your design elements for letters; carefully note the placement of the address block.

*   [Letter design templatearrow-up-right](https://s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/public.lob.com/assets/letter_template_updated+4_25.pdf)
     (for 6 sheets of paper or less)
    
*   [Letter design templatearrow-up-right](https://s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/public.lob.com/assets/letter_flat_template_updated+4_25.pdf)
     (for over 6 sheets of paper but not exceeding 60)
    

Lob standard letters cannot accommodate letters that bleed or print edge-to-edge. All text and design must be 1/16” from all sides leaving the required 1/16” clear space on all sides.

chevron-rightWhat is that small QR code printed on my letters? [hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/letters#what-is-that-small-qr-code-printed-on-my-letters)

What looks like a small QR code printed on letters is a data matrix, a type 2D barcode used by our print partners to encode information to facilitate efficient sorting, tracking, and delivery. Recipient attempts to scan this code won't direct anywhere, but cameras read these small barcodes during the automation process. Typically, this appears on the lower corner of letters (as noted on our templates), though some print partners may place it in the address block area (as shown below).

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FCy09g0n0is5N5tQpqw8Z%252Fsample2D_addressblock.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D352e2014-5947-467f-af79-6aa4797977f1&width=300&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=876d5eac&sv=2)

chevron-rightWhat is the difference between a page and a sheet of paper?[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/letters#what-is-the-difference-between-a-page-and-a-sheet-of-paper)

A "page" in the context of a Lob letter is a PDF page, while a "sheet" refers to the physical paper that is used to print the letter. Lob charges based on the length of a letter in PDF pages, while the length of a letter in sheets is used to determine what envelope a letter uses.

**8.5x14” Letters (“Legal letters”)**

circle-info

Legal letters are exclusive to Enterprise edition customers. Upgrade your [Print & Mail editionarrow-up-right](https://dashboard.lob.com/#/settings/editions)
 to gain access to this mail format, or reach out to our [sales teamarrow-up-right](https://www.lob.com/sales)
 to learn more.

circle-check

Legal letters currently have a 4-day SLA.

Lob offers 8.5x14" letters in both black & white and in color, which can be printed single or double-sided.

Using two parallel folds, a legal-sized letter is folded in half, then folded again _with a slight overlap_ (two edge panels will be slightly larger than the middle panels); this allows it to fit in a standard #10 double-windowed outer envelope. Legal letters cannot exceed 3 sheets of paper at this time.

Reference our design templates on where to place your design elements for letters; carefully note the placement of the address block.

*   [Legal letter design template arrow-up-right](https://s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/public.lob.com/assets/Legal_Letter_updated_4_25.pdf)
    (3 sheets of paper or less)
    

Lob Legal letters cannot accommodate letters that bleed or print edge-to-edge. All text and design must be 1/16” from all sides leaving the required 1/16” clear space on all sides.

chevron-rightWhat is that small QR code printed on my letters? [hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/letters#what-is-that-small-qr-code-printed-on-my-letters-1)

What looks like a small QR code printed on letters is a data matrix, a type 2D barcode used by our print partners to encode information to facilitate efficient sorting, tracking, and delivery. Recipient attempts to scan this code won't direct anywhere, but cameras read these small barcodes during the automation process. Typically, this appears on the lower corner of letters (as noted on our templates), though some print partners may place it in the address block area (as shown below).

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FCy09g0n0is5N5tQpqw8Z%252Fsample2D_addressblock.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D352e2014-5947-467f-af79-6aa4797977f1&width=300&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=876d5eac&sv=2)

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/letters#paper-specs)

Paper specs

We use a few pre-approved papers across our commercial printer network. In ensuring uniform quality and consistency across all of our mail pieces, each paper source specification must fall within a small range. The specification/value range pairs are:

*   8.5" x 11" B&W & color letters
    
    *   Basis Weight: 60# text
        
    *   C-fold letters
        
    
*   8.5" x 14" B&W & color letters
    
    *   Basis Weight: 60# text
        
    *   Double parallel fold
        
    

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/letters#address-placement-2)

Address placement

If you do not follow these address guidelines, your letter will be printed incorrectly.

The `address_placement` parameter specifies the location of the address information that will show through the double-window envelope. Options are `top_first_page` and `insert_blank_page`.

By default, Lob selects `address_placement = top_first_page`, meaning Lob will print address information at the top of your provided first page. Make sure to leave ample space for the address information to be printed, which will show through our standard double-windowed envelope. To see how this will impact your letter design, view our [letter templatearrow-up-right](https://s3-us-west-2.amazonaws.com/public.lob.com/assets/templates/letter_template.pdf)
 (6 sheets or less).

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252F4UJ6M1fAHr0vSu1oo7uh%252Fletter%25206%2520pages%2520or%2520less.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3De78bdc7e-40a4-414b-bd79-04dee0e44289&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=d92c5963&sv=2)

circle-exclamation

Letters may shift slightly within their envelope during transit. As such, we do not recommend putting any sensitive content close to the window area.

If you pass `address_placement = insert_blank_page`, a blank address page will be inserted at the beginning of your file where we will print the address information to show through our standard double-windowed envelope.

triangle-exclamation

If a blank address page is inserted, note that you _will_ be charged for the extra page. Reference the [Letter Unit Pricing section](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/ready-to-get-started/pricing-details#letters-6)
 for charges you will incur based on your tier.

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/letters#mailing-letters-3)

Logo placement

Your logo should be entirely within the blue box of the letter template, with no overlap of the red box, in order to print appropriately.

If you're looking to print a color logo then ensure you're setting the color parameter to 'true' if you would like to print in color otherwise you can set it to false.

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/letters#mailing-letters-3-1)

Letter best practices


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Ensure your letters get to their final destination by following these guidelines:

*   The `from` field is required for all letters, regardless of the destination
    
*   Letters may contain PII as envelopes have an [interior security tint](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/letter-envelopes#standard-letter-outer-envelopes-0)
     for privacy
    
*   [Mailing class](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/mailing-classes-and-postage#available-mailing-class-options-5)
     options may differ based on the destination and use case (e.g. promotional vs transactional)
    

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/letters#using-certified-mail-or-registered-mail-4)


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Self-Mailers | Lob Help Center

Self-mailers can grab attention with their creative design unfolding to tell your brand's story. They offer more room for your messaging but are folded to a compact size. Their all-in-one format eliminates the need for envelopes, encouraging immediate engagement from recipients and facilitating a quicker response.

circle-info

Self-mailers are exclusive to Enterprise edition customers. Upgrade your [Print & Mail editionarrow-up-right](https://dashboard.lob.com/#/settings/editions)
 to gain access to this mail format, or reach out to our [sales teamarrow-up-right](https://www.lob.com/sales)
 to learn more.

Check out the following resources to get started with self-mailers

*   API documentation: [Self-Mailers APIarrow-up-right](https://docs.lob.com/#tag/Self-Mailers)
     or [Campaigns APIarrow-up-right](https://docs.lob.com/#tag/Campaigns)
    
*   Lob dashboard: [Self-mailerarrow-up-right](https://dashboard.lob.com/self-mailers)
     section or [Campaignsarrow-up-right](https://dashboard.lob.com/campaigns/)
     for self-mailer campaigns
    
*   [GitHub libraryarrow-up-right](https://github.com/lob/examples/)
     for HTML templates
    
*   [Template Galleryarrow-up-right](https://www.lob.com/template-gallery#folded-self-mailers)
     for ready-to-use creatives and design inspiration
    

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/self-mailers#self-mailer-formats)

Self-mailer formats


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FFIbDeZDtSEvJy2EFffo2%252F23.10%2520Trifold%2520Chart%2520Final%2520Version.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D2f379c76-4cf6-4fa4-aa46-02c5927a7d3a&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=60e91c15&sv=2)

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/self-mailers#dimensions-and-specs-7)

Layout dimensions & specs


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/self-mailers#layout-dimensions)

Layout dimensions

Lob currently supports the following self-mailers:

*   [6x18” bifold templatearrow-up-right](https://s3-us-west-2.amazonaws.com/public.lob.com/assets/templates/self_mailers/6x18_sfm_bifold_template.pdf)
    : measures 6x9” when folded in half, no offset of panels, and unfolds horizontally
    
*   [12x9” bifold templatearrow-up-right](https://s3-us-west-2.amazonaws.com/public.lob.com/assets/templates/self_mailers/12x9_sfm_bifold_template.pdf)
    : measures 6x9” when folded in half, no offset of panels, and unfolds vertically
    
*   [11x9" bifold templatearrow-up-right](https://s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/public.lob.com/assets/templates/self_mailers/11x9_sfm_bifold_template.pdf)
    : measures 6x9" when folded in half, has a 1" offset between the top and bottom panels when folded, and only unfolds vertically
    
*   [17.75x9" trifold templatearrow-up-right](https://s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/public.lob.com/assets/Updated+9x17.75+Trifold+SFM.pdf)
    : measures 6”x9" when c-folded inward, has a .25" offset on the bottom panel when folded, and only unfolds vertically.
    

Follow our design templates carefully to ensure you place your design elements for each permutation, including the location of the adhesive, in the appropriate location. (For example, there are four panels available to customize for all bifolds, wherein one of the outside panels must have an [ink-free zone](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/self-mailers#ink-free-zone-dimensions-8)
 for the address and postage area.)

circle-exclamation

**Make sure all artwork submissions are facing upright for both inside and outside panels.** Our printers will invert the inside panel of submitted customer artwork during the production phase.

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fstatic.helpjuice.com%2Fhelpjuice_production%2Fuploads%2Fupload%2Fimage%2F9583%2Fdirect%2F1641887499459-1641887499459.png&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=8b697805&sv=2)

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/self-mailers#paper-specs)

Paper specs

We use a few pre-approved papers across our commercial printer network. In ensuring uniform quality and consistency across all of our mail pieces, each paper source specification must fall within a small range. The specification/value range pairs are listed below:

*   Basis Weight: 80# Cover with Gloss
    
*   GSM: 218
    
*   Full Bleed
    
*   1 Side UV Gloss
    

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/self-mailers#ink-free-zone-dimensions-8)

Ink-free zone dimensions

*   6x18" bifold self-mailer
    
    *   W x H: 4 x 2.375"
        
    *   0.15" from the center fold line
        
    *   0.25" from the bottom edge (including bleed)
        
    *   Placement: Left panel outside
        
    
*   12x9" bifold self-mailer
    
    *   W x H: 4 x 2.375"
        
    *   0.15" from the center fold line
        
    *   0.25" from the right edge (including bleed)
        
    *   Placement: Top panel outside
        
    
*   11x9" bifold self-mailer
    
    *   W x H: 4 x 2.375"
        
    *   0.15" from the center fold line
        
    *   0.25" from the right edge (including bleed)
        
    *   Placement: Top panel outside
        
    
*   17.75x9" trifold self-mailer
    
    *   W x H: 4 x 2.375" 0.15" from the center fold line
        
    *   0.25" from the right edge (including bleed)
        
    *   Placement: Top panel outside
        
    *   Glue zone 9”x.5” (at 12” score)
        
    

circle-info

A Carbon Neutral Mail certification will automatically be included within the official use address block on the backside for all bifold self-mailers printed by Lob. Read more about how you can send mail in a more environmentally responsible way with Lob in our [Carbon Neutral Mail](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/artboard-layout#carbon-neutral-mail-certification-9)
 section.

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/self-mailers#self-mailer-adhesives-9)

Self-mailer adhesives


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Stain-resistant, low tack, clear fugitive glue is used for adhesives on the folded self-mailers.

Glue is positioned within 0.25" of the opening edges and placed opposite the final fold. Glue is applied by one of the following methods:

*   Continuous glue line at least 0.125" wide
    
*   Three or four glue spots at least 0.375" in diameter
    
*   Three or four elongated glue lines
    

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/self-mailers#design-best-practices-10)

Self-mailer best practices


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Ensure your self-mailers get to their final destination by following these guidelines:

*   The `from` field is required for all self-mailers, regardless of the destination
    
*   Self-mailers should not contain any PII outside of the [no-ink zone](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/artboard-layout#address-block-postage-8)
    
*   Avoid any [prohibited artwork](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/artboard-layout#prohibited-artwork-11)
     to ensure your mail does not get rejected by USPS
    
*   HIPAA-compliant self-mailers are not offered at this time, as self-mailers are not placed in envelopes
    
*   [Mailing Class](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/mailing-classes-and-postage#available-mailing-class-options-5)
     options may differ based on destination and use type (e.g. promotional vs non-promotional)
    
*   We do not recommend placing promotional address information in your creative in the bottom 2.375" of a mailpiece opposite the address panel. This will prevent the USPS from accidentally scanning the wrong side of the mailpiece. [Learn more here.](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/artboard-layout#address-zone-warning)
    

[PreviousPostcardschevron-left](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/postcards)
[NextLetterschevron-right](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/letters)

Last updated 11 months ago

# Informed Delivery | Lob Help Center

As USPS’ Mail Service Provider of the year, Lob works in lockstep with the postal service to bring you solutions to optimize your direct mail performance.

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/maximizing-engagement/informed-delivery#what-is-informed-delivery)

What is Informed Delivery?


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Informed Deliveryarrow-up-right](https://www.usps.com/manage/informed-delivery.htm)
 by USPS enhances the mail experience by providing subscribers a daily email with a digital preview of incoming mail.

The preview may be a representative image or an actual scan of the mailpiece. The email includes a ["ride-along" image](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/maximizing-engagement/informed-delivery#what-makes-a-great-ride-along-image)
: a small, full-color, interactive image that redirects to a target URL. This supplemental content is meant to enhance the customer call-to-action and reinforce the messaging and objective of the mailpiece.

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FuB0GgvLKlfCCPoYh2v6h%252FInformed_Delivery_sample.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D8418855c-6809-461a-80d1-5657db157097&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=e1e44367&sv=2)

_Image source:_ [_2024 Informed Delivery Guidebook_arrow-up-right](https://postalpro.usps.com/promotions/ID?_gl=1*19newo6*_gcl_au*MjAxMjE3Mjk3Ni4xNzE2NDczNTI3*_ga*MjExNDg2NzE5OC4xNzE2NDczNTI3*_ga_3NXP3C8S9V*MTcxNzUxODkwNi4zLjAuMTcxNzUxODkwNi4wLjAuMA..)

You can review the [USPS's 2024 Informed Delivery Guidebook herearrow-up-right](https://postalpro.usps.com/promotions/ID?_gl=1*19newo6*_gcl_au*MjAxMjE3Mjk3Ni4xNzE2NDczNTI3*_ga*MjExNDg2NzE5OC4xNzE2NDczNTI3*_ga_3NXP3C8S9V*MTcxNzUxODkwNi4zLjAuMTcxNzUxODkwNi4wLjAuMA..)
[.arrow-up-right](https://postalpro.usps.com/promotions/ID?_gl=1*19newo6*_gcl_au*MjAxMjE3Mjk3Ni4xNzE2NDczNTI3*_ga*MjExNDg2NzE5OC4xNzE2NDczNTI3*_ga_3NXP3C8S9V*MTcxNzUxODkwNi4zLjAuMTcxNzUxODkwNi4wLjAuMA..)

circle-info

Access to this feature is exclusive to Enterprise plan customers, and by request only. Upgrade to the appropriate [Print & Mail Editionarrow-up-right](https://dashboard.lob.com/#/settings/editions)
 to gain access.

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/maximizing-engagement/informed-delivery#why-should-mailers-use-informed-delivery)

Why should mailers use Informed Delivery?


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Direct mail helps your brand increase awareness, connect with potential customers, and drive conversions. However, combining this marketing method with digital technologies can lead to even more successful results. Using Informed Delivery is one of the best ways to do this.

While there is no guarantee everyone in your audience has opted into the Informed Delivery program, the list continues to grow. Per USPS, as of March of this year, the Informed Delivery user base has grown to 62.4M active users!

*   **Increased Customer Engagement:** Integrates physical mail with digital campaigns.
    
*   **Additional Digital Footprint:** Extends marketing reach at no additional cost.
    
*   **Attribution Data:** Provides additional insights into mail campaign performance.
    

circle-check

**Cost Savings:** 3-4% postage discount for approved campaigns during the [USPS promotional period](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/maximizing-engagement/informed-delivery#how-can-i-take-advantage-of-the-informed-delivery-promotion-period-august-1-december-31-2024)
, August 1-December 31, 2024. Reach out to your Account Manager to learn more.

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/maximizing-engagement/informed-delivery#step-1-sign-up-for-informed-delivery-promotion)

Step 1: Sign up for Informed Delivery Promotion


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This process is necessary regardless of how a user will execute the campaign.

1.  Log into the Lob Dashboard; navigate to Billing > Promotions. Click on "Create an Informed Delivery Promotion."
    

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FXT24IO6bH4VKaLOAuJGU%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D6ab5ac13-fa03-4c69-9009-4836849c931a&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=bdcb164&sv=2)

1.  Indicate the Promotion Name
    
2.  Input the Promotion URL
    
3.  Upload your [Ride-along image](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/maximizing-engagement/informed-delivery#what-makes-a-great-ride-along-image)
    
    circle-exclamation
    
    Ride-along image specifications: **Must be full color**, JPG, 200x300px, max file size of 200KB.
    

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252F2krTFN4uixOCReNjXFnW%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Df0d4c319-d538-49e1-a918-1d3c374da728&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=3ffef2a6&sv=2)

1.  Click "Create an Informed Delivery Promotion" to send your promotion application to USPS for approval.
    
2.  You will receive an email notification once your promotion is approved. (Please allow for 3-5 days approval time.)
    

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/maximizing-engagement/informed-delivery#step-2-send-an-informed-delivery-campaign)

Step 2: Send an Informed Delivery campaign


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Once approved, you can send an Informed Delivery campaign with postcards, letters, self-mailers, and checks.

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/maximizing-engagement/informed-delivery#send-via-api)

Send via API

1.  Use the [Lob APIarrow-up-right](https://docs.lob.com/#tag/Informed-Delivery-Campaign/operation/informed_delivery_campaign_create)
     to POST `informed_delivery_campaign` and get a `campaign_id`.
    

*   Include required fields: [Ride-Along Image](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/maximizing-engagement/informed-delivery#what-makes-a-great-ride-along-image)
    , URL, Campaign Size.
    
    circle-exclamation
    
    Ride-along image specifications: **Must be full color**, JPG, 200x300px, max file size of 200KB.
    
*   _Optionally_, include
    
    *   Brand Name: If you do not pass a brand name, it will auto-populate in the Informed Delivery email as the Company name in Lob's system.
        
    *   Representative image: A sample image of your mailpiece. This photo should represent all mailers in the Informed Delivery Campaign, and will be sent to all Informed Delivery subscribers; avoid using any PII. Campaigns with no representative image will display a USPS scan of the mailpiece.
        
        circle-check
        
        Representative image specifications: JPG, 500x780px, max file size of 200KB.
        
    

1.  On successful POST, receive a `campaign_id` and validation if it qualifies for an existing promotion.
    
2.  Once validated, send your mail as usual, including the `usps_campaign_id` in your submission.
    

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/maximizing-engagement/informed-delivery#send-via-campaigns)

Send via Campaigns

1.  Begin your campaign setup as usual in Lob Campaigns.
    
2.  When prompted, select the option to use Informed Delivery.
    
3.  Choose from available approved Informed Delivery Promotions (Procured in [Step 1](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/maximizing-engagement/informed-delivery#step-1-sign-up-for-informed-delivery-promotion)
    ).
    
4.  Fill out required information:
    

*   Promotional URL
    
*   Upload the [Ride-Along Image](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/maximizing-engagement/informed-delivery#what-makes-a-great-ride-along-image)
    
    circle-exclamation
    
    Ride-along image specifications: JPG, 500x780px, max file size of 200KB.
    

1.  _Optionally,_ input:
    

*   Brand Name: This populates in the Informed Delivery email sent to the end user; if you do not update this field, it will default to your company name in the Lob database.
    
*   Representative image: Upload a sample image of your mailpiece. This photo should represent all mailers in the Informed Delivery campaign, and will be sent to all Informed Delivery subscribers; avoid using any PII. Campaigns with no representative image will display a USPS scan of the mailpiece.
    

circle-check

Representative image specifications: JPG, 500x780px, max file size of 200KB.

1.  Please look over the uploaded images and make sure all information is correct.
    

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FEVnwDNJzM7PJuD5AD1Xs%252FInformedDeliverySendCampaigns.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D002a9d58-3402-4a54-b929-97d52179ebf2&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=35c4481a&sv=2)

1.  Click "Next Step" to proceed with the Campaigns worklflow.
    
2.  Click "Place Order" as you normally would to finalize the campaign.
    

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/maximizing-engagement/informed-delivery#informed-delivery-engagement-metrics)

Informed Delivery engagement metrics


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Engagement metrics for Informed Delivery campaigns will be visible in the Mail Analytics in the dashboard, under the [Engagement arrow-up-right](https://dashboard.lob.com/analytics/attribution-analytics)
tab. Learn more about [Mail Analytics here](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/getting-data-and-results/mail-analytics)
. Below is a breakdown of the engagement metrics:

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FMZBx6SuJ5Bl93jN9mJM5%252FInformedDeliveryAnalytics.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Dd7e1a225-e82e-4f4f-a2a8-f5c82157f9f0&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=5f4eab24&sv=2)

_**Metric**_

_**Description**_

**Mail Pieces**

Total number of mail pieces sent as part of your Informed Delivery Campaign.

**Emails**

The number of recipients signed up for Informed Delivery who received an Informed Delivery email.

**Emails Opened**

The number of emails that were opened. _Note: This number can exceed 100% as multiple people within the same household may receive and open their Informed Delivery email._

**Click Through**

The total number of times the link in your Informed Delivery campaign was clicked.

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/maximizing-engagement/informed-delivery#informed-delivery-faqs)

Informed Delivery FAQs


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/maximizing-engagement/informed-delivery#what-makes-a-great-ride-along-image)

What makes a great ride-along image?

Note your image **must** be in full-color; the USPS has provided more helpful guidelines here: [https://www.usps.com/business/pdf/informed-delivery-campaign-elements-guide.pdfarrow-up-right](https://www.usps.com/business/pdf/informed-delivery-campaign-elements-guide.pdf)

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/maximizing-engagement/informed-delivery#how-can-i-take-advantage-of-the-informed-delivery-promotion-period-august-1-december-31-2024)

How can I take advantage of the Informed Delivery promotion period August 1- December 31, 2024?

Reach out to your Account Manager today!

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/maximizing-engagement/informed-delivery#can-i-use-informed-delivery-for-an-automated-campaign)

Can I use Informed Delivery for an Automated Campaign?

Not yet; this is on our roadmap.

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/maximizing-engagement/informed-delivery#how-long-can-an-informed-delivery-campaign-run-for)

How long can an Informed Delivery campaign run for?

45 days.

# International AV suite | Lob Help Center

Lob's Address Verification API provides a comprehensive solution for businesses looking to optimize their international customer experiences and make more informed decisions about their international address data.

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/address-verification/ready-to-start-av/international-av-suite#international-verifications-5)

International verifications


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Validate, automatically correct, and standardize non-US international addresses. See our [API referencearrow-up-right](https://docs.lob.com/#tag/Intl-Verifications)
 for details.

We follow [USPS standardsarrow-up-right](http://pe.usps.com/text/imm/immc1_008.htm)
 for sending international mail. Therefore, any addresses submitted in a language other than English will see the address returned in English.

chevron-rightSupported international countries[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/address-verification/ready-to-start-av/international-av-suite#supported-international-countries)

See our most up-to-date list of [global address coveragearrow-up-right](https://www.lob.com/global-address-coverage)
 for our International Address Verification API.

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/address-verification/ready-to-start-av/international-av-suite#international-deliverability-insights-1)

International deliverability insights


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Lob's International Deliverability provides greater visibility into an address’s delivery response so customers can make more informed decisions about how they manage international address data. Customers are able to understand when a portion of the address has been corrected and if it has resolved the deliverability issues. To leverage this data, review the complete [API documentationarrow-up-right](https://docs.lob.com/#operation/intl_verification)
 under `Status`.

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/address-verification/ready-to-start-av/international-av-suite#international-autocomplete-7)

International autocomplete


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

International address autocompletion for non-US addresses is not currently available. If you believe this feature is integral to your business, please reach out to [support](https://help.lob.com/resources/getting-support)
 [envelope](https://help.lob.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#8ffcfaffffe0fdfbcfe3e0eda1ece0e2)
to see if we can support your needs.

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/address-verification/ready-to-start-av/international-av-suite#configurable-transliteration-0)

Configurable transliteration


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Configurable Transliteration provides Address Verification customers greater flexibility and customization when operating around the world. Lob ensures that addresses are correct and deliverable while providing optionality for the address response to be in the native language. This means a customer is able to provide cart checkout messaging that adapts based on where the end customer is located thus creating a more personalized experience. It also means that when working with foreign languages with non-Latin characters the data is seamlessly transliterated and mapped for normalized data storage.

To customize your language response, review the complete [API documentationarrow-up-right](https://docs.lob.com/#operation/bulk_intl_verifications)
 under `x-lang-output`.

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/address-verification/ready-to-start-av/international-av-suite#global-av-data-coverage-2)

Global AV data coverage


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Country Coverage equips Lob customers with the details needed to make an informed decision about a deliverable address and the potential for false positives. The International Address Verification API provides validation to varying degrees of granularity depending on the country the address is in. Country Coverage indicates the level to which an address has been verified. To leverage this information, review the [API documentationarrow-up-right](https://docs.lob.com/#operation/intl_verification)
 under `Coverage`.

View our most updated [Global Address Coveragearrow-up-right](https://www.lob.com/global-address-coverage)
 here.

[PreviousUS AV product suitechevron-left](https://help.lob.com/address-verification/ready-to-start-av/us-av-product-suite)
[NextAV pricingchevron-right](https://help.lob.com/address-verification/ready-to-start-av/av-pricing)

# Declaring mail use type | Lob Help Center

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/managing-mail-settings/declaring-mail-use-type#overview)

Overview


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Identifying your mail **use type** helps Lob populate the right mail settings and postage options to ensure your mail is produced and delivered in an optimal way. Lob requires that you identify—or tag—your mail with one of the following use type options:

*   **Marketing mail**: Any mailers that are sent for marketing, advertising, and promotional purposes
    
*   **Operational mail**: All other mail, typically transactional or functional in nature, such as invoices, adverse action notices, statements, and other confidential mail that include sensitive PII/PHI data
    

circle-check

It will be your full responsibility to accurately represent the purpose of mail that is created and sent. Lob will be unable to make any determination of which use type should be applied to your mail being sent, or for making retroactive changes to your API calls.

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/managing-mail-settings/declaring-mail-use-type#how-to-declare-your-mail-use-type)

How to declare your mail use type


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You should declare your mail’s use type at the appropriate level based on your organization's usage (at the account, campaign, or mailpiece level).

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/managing-mail-settings/declaring-mail-use-type#id-1-setting-use-type-as-an-account-default-3)

1\. Setting use type for an account (default)

If the majority of your mailpieces have a singular use type, then it is best to configure a default use type at the account level. This can be changed by an account administrator in the Use Type section in the [Accounts Tab in the dashboard settingsarrow-up-right](https://dashboard.lob.com/settings/account)
. **An account-level setting will be applied to any mailpiece created without a** `**use_type**` **at the individual mailpiece or campaign level.**

circle-exclamation

While an account-level designation is not required, the failure to declare a mail use type at _any_ level means that in the absence of a default value, your organization will be prevented from sending any mail until a use type is declared.

To set an account-level default, go to your [Dashboard Settings > Accountsarrow-up-right](https://dashboard.lob.com/settings/account)
 tab:

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fstatic.helpjuice.com%2Fhelpjuice_production%2Fuploads%2Fupload%2Fimage%2F9583%2F2094102%2FScreen_Shot_2022-07-05_at_5.32.22_PM.png&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=b7cefaf6&sv=2)

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/managing-mail-settings/declaring-mail-use-type#id-2-setting-use-types-for-each-mailpiece-4)

2\. Setting use types for each mailpiece

If you or your team sends mailpieces with varying use types (including different teams that utilize the same Lob account for different purposes), then it is best to declare your use types at the single mailpiece level or at the campaign level.

If you have built a native Lob integration to trigger individual mailpieces, you will want to configure a use type at the individual mailpiece level. **A mailpiece designation will override your account-level use type, regardless of whether the default selection is made.**

#### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/managing-mail-settings/declaring-mail-use-type#in-the-dashboard)

**In the Dashboard**

When sending a single mailpiece through the dashboard, use type will be a required selection for every mailpiece format ([lettersarrow-up-right](https://dashboard.lob.com/letters/create)
, [postcardsarrow-up-right](https://dashboard.lob.com/postcards/create)
, [self-mailersarrow-up-right](https://dashboard.lob.com/self-mailers/create)
, or [checksarrow-up-right](https://dashboard.lob.com/checks/create)
).

**Example:** Use Type selection when creating a new [postcardarrow-up-right](https://dashboard.lob.com/postcards/create)
 in the Dashboard

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Flh3.googleusercontent.com%2FlU3KZtIBL1i7ex8hQ7oSaJmLdeS-M1qAaDsAoL8_9tYfpSc2Nd-Jz8WS9GiVt673q9L0yViRCQc-Cz9N1bKFwPg9Lj7BU8NKKovIQVkH2QSBTmObLYSOO0aOHoNzSyYN-DcOp-oWlrDtaW_XV8Mw6Y4kTvbiY1JJoMPryBTI7R1KPLI9x-M3-7WFtwbQ8BMU%3Dnw&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=7ba46875&sv=2)

#### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/managing-mail-settings/declaring-mail-use-type#in-the-api)

**In the API**

When sending a `POST` request to the below endpoints, pass in a `use_type` value of either `marketing` or `operational`. Note that the account default will be overridden by passing in a `use_type` for each mailpiece. If no account default is set and no single endpoint-level `use_type` is passed in the request, the mailpiece cannot be created and will fail with a 422 error.

`api.lob.com/v1/letters api.lob.com/v1/postcards api.lob.com/v1/self_mailers api.lob.com/v1/checks`

Example: `POST /v1/postcards` request with `use_type` included

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/managing-mail-settings/declaring-mail-use-type#id-3-setting-use-types-for-each-campaign-7)

3\. Setting use types for each campaign

The Campaigns product is designed to help send large volumes of mail quickly, either through the [dashboardarrow-up-right](https://dashboard.lob.com/campaigns)
 or [Campaigns APIarrow-up-right](https://docs.lob.com/#tag/Campaigns)
.

If you have built a native Lob integration to send mail campaigns, you will want to configure a use type at the campaign level. **A campaign-level designation will override your account-level mail use type, regardless of whether the default selection is made.**

#### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/managing-mail-settings/declaring-mail-use-type#in-the-dashboard-1)

**In the Dashboard**

When sending a Campaign on the dashboard, selecting a campaign use type is a required step during the “[Configure campaignarrow-up-right](https://dashboard.lob.com/campaigns/create/settings)
” page in Step 1. You will be able to select one of two options: Marketing or Operational. Any selection will be applied to all mailpieces within the same campaign.

Example:

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Flh3.googleusercontent.com%2F6fyIxk7fS35qtckIh6UByNcQf9owJyzzDvBibsIbyDA3gpHBlZFvvlbdC1B_DicLE_pMjsAgjSdSkrEyqvzwejis1I7CoyrVRfb9a8yu5XgMI0HdwYi0EPzKjS70iHtPFbQeNBVouLYJhPVeFLE_RKvFExAv0xnT1MXLOV4r0o3zyRuqmEv30Rf058HQvVWC%3Dnw&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=8e43d49e&sv=2)

#### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/managing-mail-settings/declaring-mail-use-type#in-the-api-1)

**In the API**

When using the [Campaigns APIarrow-up-right](https://docs.lob.com/#tag/Campaigns)
, add the `use_type` field to the `POST /v1/campaigns` request. If the `use_type` is not added, the campaign will use the account default `use_type`. If no account default is set and no campaign-level `use_type` is passed in the request, the campaign cannot be created and will fail with a 422 error.

Example: `POST /v1/campaigns` request with `use_type` included

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/managing-mail-settings/declaring-mail-use-type#error-reference)

Error reference


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If no account default is set and no campaign or mailpiece `use_type` field is passed in your API requests, your calls will fail with a 422 error.

Read more about [error codesarrow-up-right](https://docs.lob.com/#tag/Errors)
 under the "Error Codes - Advanced" section of our API Reference.

triangle-exclamation

To ensure your mail sends do not fail in the future, we strongly recommend you **set an** **account default** as a fall-back option as soon as possible

[PreviousUsing metadatachevron-left](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/managing-mail-settings/using-metadata)
[NextUSPS Promotions Through Lobchevron-right](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/usps-promotions-through-lob)

Last updated 3 years ago

Was this helpful?

*   [How to declare your mail use type](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/managing-mail-settings/declaring-mail-use-type#how-to-declare-your-mail-use-type)
    
*   [1\. Setting use type for an account (default)](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/managing-mail-settings/declaring-mail-use-type#id-1-setting-use-type-as-an-account-default-3)
