# User settings | Lob Help Center

View and adjust user-level settings in the [Team tabarrow-up-right](https://dashboard.lob.com/#/settings/team)
 in your dashboard; these can also be viewed by selecting the user name (bottom right of left navigation menu), then User Settings.

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/account-management/user-settings#user-roles-permissions-0)

User roles & permissions


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

There are three types of user permission levels available that can be provided to any Lob account user:

*   **Administrator** gives unrestricted access to everything. You can see API keys, update payment information, invite new users and edit existing users, manage custom inventory, etc. By default, the first creator of the Lob account is an Administrator.
    
*   **Read & Write** lets users use the API and view past API requests, but does not allow access to certain account information. Read & Write users cannot view or update payment information, change pricing plans, edit or delete other team members, or edit company information.
    
*   **Read Only** only lets users view past API requests. Read Only users cannot see API keys, create test or live API requests, upgrade your API Version, view or update payment information, change pricing plans, edit, delete, or invite other team members, or edit company information.
    

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/account-management/user-settings#inviting-team-members-1)

Inviting team members


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Using the "+ Add Team Member" button at the bottom of the page in the [Team tabarrow-up-right](https://dashboard.lob.com/#/settings/team)
, you can add team members to join your Lob account. All users on an account share the same API keys and payment method.

The maximum number of users you can add to your account is dictated by your current [Print & Mail editionarrow-up-right](https://dashboard.lob.com/#/settings/editions)
. Depending on which Edition, you may only have the ability to create Read & Write and Read Only users.

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fstatic.helpjuice.com%2Fhelpjuice_production%2Fuploads%2Fupload%2Fimage%2F9583%2Fdirect%2F1648630276743-Screen%2520Shot%25202022-03-30%2520at%25201.50.59%2520AM.png&width=300&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=bc50990a&sv=2)

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/account-management/user-settings#other-user-actions-2)

Other user actions


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   To **edit** user permissions, click the pencil icon ![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fstatic.helpjuice.com%2Fhelpjuice_production%2Fuploads%2Fupload%2Fimage%2F9583%2Fdirect%2F1643761980924-1643761980924.png&width=300&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=2147a1e1&sv=2) for that user.
    
*   To **remove** a user, click the trash icon![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fstatic.helpjuice.com%2Fhelpjuice_production%2Fuploads%2Fupload%2Fimage%2F9583%2Fdirect%2F1643839984559-1643839984559.png&width=300&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=2a92815&sv=2) for that user.
    
*   To **activate** pending users, send another email by clicking on the "Resend Invite" button for the user in question. Users must accept their invitation and log in to join your team.
    
*   To **re-invite** a deleted user to your account, reach out to support at [\[email protected\]envelope](https://help.lob.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#d2a1a7a2a2bda0a692bebdb0fcb1bdbf)
     with your Account ID.
    
*   To **add** users to your team who've already created separate accounts, contact [\[email protected\]envelope](https://help.lob.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#97e4e2e7e7f8e5e3d7fbf8f5b9f4f8fa)
     with your Account IDs.
    

[PreviousAccount-level data logschevron-left](https://help.lob.com/account-management/account-settings/account-level-data-logs)
[NextBillingchevron-right](https://help.lob.com/account-management/billing)

# Checks | Lob Help Center

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/checks#checks-overview-0)

Checks overview


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To get started with sending checks, you'll need to [create a bank account](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/checks#create-a-new-bank-account)
 to originate checks from. You will also identify which [check template](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/checks#check-dimensions-specs-1)
 and [signature image](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/checks#signature-image-file-7)
 you want associated with the account, then Lob will [verify your account](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/checks#verifying-your-bank-account-4)
. This process is outlined below.

**Just looking for check specs?** Skip ahead to [Check dimensions & specs](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/checks#check-dimensions-specs-1-1)
 here.

More resources: Once you have setup your Bank Account, visit our [Checks API documentationarrow-up-right](https://docs.lob.com/#tag/Checks)
, [GitHub libraryarrow-up-right](https://github.com/lob/examples/)
, or our [Template Galleryarrow-up-right](https://www.lob.com/template-gallery#checks)
 for ideas on how to get started.

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/checks#create-a-new-bank-account)

Create a new Bank Account


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In the dashboard, see the Bank Accounts section (under Print & Mail):

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252F05izBvQvs2u4bol5MDTy%252FNewBankAccount.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D4fa6690c-c8f3-47e8-ba9e-8ae4aff53cb8&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=a2bdd3a7&sv=2)

You can also add a bank account [via our Bank Accounts APIarrow-up-right](https://docs.lob.com/#tag/Bank-Accounts)
 but note you are unable to upload a signatory image via this method.

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/checks#check-dimensions-specs-1)

Check templates


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In the “Bank Accounts” section of your dashboard (under Print & Mail) you will select which template you would like to associate with your bank under the “Check Template Type”.

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FjWPIwg5zTqHNv8YyZ4bT%252FCheckTemplateType.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Dd617ba4e-b4c5-40c9-8e18-655fe504ac80&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=efc22e3d&sv=2)

We offer two options when it comes to check templates:

**Common Check:** This is the universal check we have offered that is accepted at the majority of banks across the US.

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252F0zOHfuq9TWEPyc90yoWT%252FCheck2.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Da213a0af-8058-4335-b1b0-c5995266ae9c&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=2dc2894&sv=2)

Common check

**JPM Check:** This is the JPM check template that is approved by JP Morgan Chase and utilizes Positive Pay.

circle-info

If you are using the JPM Check template, you will need to add additional information (Bank City, State, Zip, and Fractional Routing Number).

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FB1I9uHDpiuVpVs3rzI1u%252FCheckJPM.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D8669dde8-c9b2-4d0e-8413-d22cec6c2d1d&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=8e2c091b&sv=2)

JPM check

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/checks#check-with-your-bank-3)

Check with your bank

Check with your bank to make sure that the check will be accepted. A new check template or different signatory can cause the bank to flag your account. The best way to avoid this happening is to let your bank know that the checks are valid.

Your money is not transferred to Lob. When the payee deposits the check that was sent through Lob's Checks API, it will pull money directly from the specified bank account into the recipient's bank account.

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/checks#uploading-a-signature-for-checks-6)

Uploading a signature for checks


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

One of the options for associating a custom signature to a bank account, so that it can be printed on checks, is uploading an image file when you create a Bank Account in the Lob dashboard.

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FXRl54RILQSTqgUghe95R%252FAdd%2520Signature.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D513dc065-26e5-4a09-8280-934c0902d336&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=a96fe52f&sv=2)

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/checks#signature-image-file-7)

Signature image file

The uploaded signature will be printed on all Lob checks originating from that specific bank account. Make sure to download the digital signature and authorize its use with your bank once your bank account is created.

If you would like to upload a new custom image file as a signature anytime after the initial creation of the bank account, you must reach out to [\[email protected\]envelope](https://help.lob.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#8bf8fefbfbe4f9ffcbe7e4e9a5e8e4e6)
 with:

*   The `bank_account_id` of the bank account that corresponds with the signature
    
*   A PNG of the signature (the background of the image must be transparent)
    

circle-info

When an image for the signature isn’t provided, we implement a cursive font rendering of the signatory’s name instead.

Below are the guidelines to adhere to when prepping your image so that the signature can best be extracted and processed.

#### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/checks#image-format)

**Image format**

The image must be in either a JPEG or PNG format (.jpg, .jpeg, .png). The background of the image must be transparent.

#### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/checks#dimensions)

**Dimensions**

The image must be at least 330px x 105px. The image may be larger but we recommend a width to height ratio of approximately 3:1 for best results.

#### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/checks#color-and-size)

**Color and size**

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fstatic.helpjuice.com%2Fhelpjuice_production%2Fuploads%2Fupload%2Fimage%2F9583%2Fdirect%2F1641887754228-1641887754228.png&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=3bbd9a15&sv=2)

The signature must be written in black or dark ink on a white or light background. The signature should be centered and should occupy most of the image.

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/checks#things-to-avoid-11)

Things to avoid

Here are a few of the most common mistakes that we recommend you avoid when preparing a signature image.

circle-exclamation

**DON'T: Have the signature too small in your uploaded image.**

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fstatic.helpjuice.com%2Fhelpjuice_production%2Fuploads%2Fupload%2Fimage%2F9583%2Fdirect%2F1641887361556-1641887361556.png&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=708dd105&sv=2)

Make sure to center your signature and crop your image as needed so that the signature takes up a majority of the space.

circle-exclamation

**DON'T: Write your signature on ruled or patterned paper.**

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fstatic.helpjuice.com%2Fhelpjuice_production%2Fuploads%2Fupload%2Fimage%2F9583%2Fdirect%2F1641887773971-1641887773971.png&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=a532c121&sv=2)

Write your signature on a white or light-colored background to ensure proper signature processing. Using ruled or patterned paper will likely result in unintended artifacts.

circle-exclamation

**DON'T: Include other objects or artifacts in your image other than the signature.**

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fstatic.helpjuice.com%2Fhelpjuice_production%2Fuploads%2Fupload%2Fimage%2F9583%2Fdirect%2F1641887785050-1641887785050.png&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=2df6de61&sv=2)

If you are taking a photo of your signature rather than using a scanned image, make sure to prepare your photo so that all artifacts and non-signature objects (e.g. tables, pens, dark shadows) are cropped out.

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/checks#verifying-your-bank-account-4)

Verifying your bank account


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Lob will send two micro-deposits, ranging from 1 - 100 cents, to the specified bank account in order to verify the account. You'll be asked to enter them in the dashboard or submit them through the API before you can send out any checks.

This is especially important if you are sending checks on behalf of your customers. You must verify all your customers' bank accounts before sending checks from them. This is most easily done through the API.

triangle-exclamation

Lob's Check service only works with US banks, based on US banking standards and formats. We do not support foreign bank accounts at this time.

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fstatic.helpjuice.com%2Fhelpjuice_production%2Fuploads%2Fupload%2Fimage%2F9583%2Fdirect%2F1648941588842-1648941588842.png&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=3c558d49&sv=2)

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/checks#fraudulent-activity-5)

Fraudulent activity

Our security team continuously reviews newly-created accounts. New accounts that trigger a flag will be immediately suspended and required to provide additional verification.

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/checks#limitations)

Limitations

circle-info

If you send a high volume of checks, send checks in very high dollar amounts, or you are sending HIPPA data, you may need to upgrade your [Print & Mail editionarrow-up-right](https://dashboard.lob.com/#/settings/editions)
 to gain access to this form factor, or reach out to our [sales teamarrow-up-right](https://www.lob.com/sales)
 to learn more.

If you have any questions or concerns about the safe-guards we have in place around the use of checks, please [contact usarrow-up-right](https://www.lob.com/contact)
.

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/checks#check-dimensions-specs-1-1)

Check dimensions & specs


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The specifications for Lob's check product are as follows:

*   Measures 8.5 x 3.625" in size
    
*   Contains warning bands, void indication, and security weaver on backer
    
*   [Paperarrow-up-right](https://s3-us-west-2.amazonaws.com/public.lob.com/assets/templates/check_bottom_template.pdf)
     is perforated with the bottom ⅔ of the page available for the sender to customize with artwork
    
*   Uses fugitive ink to provide additional protection against duplication of the document
    
*   Includes a prismatic multicolored background with subtle graduations to make copying and reproducing difficult
    
*   Produced with controlled stock, which ensures the security of the mail piece at every stage of production. The Padlock Icon printed on the checks ensures that the product meets or exceeds industry guidelines
    
*   Mailed in a #10 white outer envelope (double-windowed)
    

circle-info

Note that logos and branding can be added to checks via an [API requestarrow-up-right](https://docs.lob.com/#operation/check_create)
, but cannot be added through the dashboard. For more details on the space your logo can take up, visit our [API documentationarrow-up-right](https://docs.lob.com/#operation/check_create)
.

Check front

Check back

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FipQyddGyQ09pz1crAVNN%252FScreenshot%25202023-03-02%2520at%25202.15.11%2520PM.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D1aa8f798-33bd-4481-a1a3-606bf138d4ab&width=300&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=d5b3f886&sv=2)

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252Fo07wD3NglcOzr6B5imyg%252FScreenshot%25202023-03-02%2520at%25202.15.40%2520PM.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Dff9b2d67-2b27-4139-a453-36ed61746d76&width=300&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=964d6f45&sv=2)

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/checks#customizing-check-pages-12)

Customizing check pages


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can choose to leverage the blank space at the **bottom of the check** by using `check_bottom` to add any branded artwork or personalization, even if these check pages themselves do not require any artwork in order to be sendable.

It is also possible to send a letter as an **attachment** with your check (but not the other way around) by using the `attachment` string.

If you choose to customize this optional bottom section of the check, be sure to follow the provided template and leave room for the actual check itself; otherwise you risk invalidating the check. You may also add up to 5 sheets (10 double-sided pages) of optional attachment pages.

See our [Checks API documentationarrow-up-right](https://docs.lob.com/#tag/Checks)
 for more details on the `check_bottom` and `attachment` parameters, or see the [Template Galleryarrow-up-right](https://www.lob.com/template-gallery)
 for design inspiration.

*   [Check bottomarrow-up-right](https://s3-us-west-2.amazonaws.com/public.lob.com/assets/templates/check_bottom_template.pdf)
     (optional)
    
*   [Check attachmentarrow-up-right](https://s3-us-west-2.amazonaws.com/public.lob.com/assets/templates/check_attachment_template.pdf)
     (optional)
    

circle-exclamation

Check bottoms and check attachments will **always** be printed in black and white.

Check bottom template

Customized check example

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fstatic.helpjuice.com%2Fhelpjuice_production%2Fuploads%2Fupload%2Fimage%2F9583%2Fdirect%2F1641887927250-1641887927250.jpeg&width=300&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=a89ca14a&sv=2)

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FO92mSe9WIAPOQUmpxpIJ%252FScreenshot%25202023-04-12%2520at%25202.15.20%2520PM.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Dba9253fb-9deb-426b-a126-4943fd240aa9&width=300&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=4ee0d076&sv=2)

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/checks#mailing-checks-2)

Mailing checks


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Ensure your checks get to their final destination by following these guidelines:

*   The `from` field is required for all checks, regardless of the destination
    
*   Checks can only be mailed using [First Class Mail](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/mailing-classes-and-postage)
     (takes 4 to 6 business days)
    
*   USPS [Standard Class](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/mailing-classes-and-postage)
     Mail (Marketing Mail) and [Certified Mail](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/mailing-classes-and-postage/certified-mail-or-registered-mail)
     **are not available for checks**
    
*   Overnight mailing options are **not available for checks**
    

[PreviousLetter add-onschevron-left](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/letter-add-ons)
[NextSnap packschevron-right](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/designing-mail-creatives/mail-piece-design-specs/snap-packs)

Last updated 1 year ago

Was this helpful?

# Data retention | Lob Help Center

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/resources/data-retention#mailpiece-data)

Mailpiece data

Beginning September 1, 2022, Lob’s standard (or “default”) policy will be to retain mailpiece data for a period of ninety (90) days.

This means that all data specific to a particular mailpiece will be deleted once it is more than 90 days old. The default retention policy is aimed at maintaining regulatory compliance, and ensuring data security and privacy.

circle-info

Lob stores mailpiece data for a minimum of thirty-three (33) days, which is necessary for billing purposes.

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/resources/data-retention#api-logs)

API logs

As they are intended for simple, real-time debugging, Lob's logs of requests and responses to and by our API (API Logs) are retained for 30 days. Similar to mailpiece data, we provide customers the option to export this log data before it expires using the "Export" button at the top of the page of the [API Logs sectionarrow-up-right](https://dashboard.lob.com/logs)
 in the dashboard.

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/resources/data-retention#deleted-data)

Deleted data

Deleted data cannot be recovered. It is not possible to recover data after it has been deleted, which means it cannot be accessed or “undeleted.” Deleted mailpieces cannot be browsed for, and will not appear in search results.

circle-exclamation

Should you need to retain mailpiece data for legal, regulatory, or compliance purposes, you may [download it](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/getting-data-and-results/exporting-mail-data)
 before the 90-day retention period passes.

**We** **delete** **data** associated with:

*   checks
    
*   letters
    
*   postcards
    
*   self-mailers
    
*   any inline address data related to the above
    

**We** **DO NOT delete**:

*   campaign data (metadata, results, etc)
    
*   unsent mailpieces
    
*   mail tracking data
    
*   mail analytics data
    
*   addresses created using Addresses API
    

For more information regarding Lob’s Data Retention Policy, please reach out to your Account Manager, or email [\[email protected\]envelope](https://help.lob.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#9ceff9ffe9eef5e8e5dcf0f3feb2fff3f1)
.

[PreviousGetting supportchevron-left](https://help.lob.com/resources/getting-support)
[NextSustainabilitychevron-right](https://help.lob.com/resources/sustainability)

Last updated 1 year ago

*   [Deleted data](https://help.lob.com/resources/data-retention#deleted-data)

# USPS Mail Growth Incentive | Lob Help Center

Lob is committed to helping you maximize your ROI by taking advantage of every opportunity to save on postage costs.

While many USPS programs are available, a primary focus for many of our customers is the **USPS Mail Growth Incentive**. This program offers 30% credit on incremental volume that exceeds your 2025 baseline in the form of a postage credit for use on future mailings, providing a powerful way to scale your outreach while lowering your bottom line.

**How We Work Together**

It is important to note that the **USPS requires customers to sign up for the Mail Growth Incentive independently** through the USPS Business Customer Gateway. However, you aren't in this alone. Lob is here to provide the support and insight you need to navigate the enrollment process, meet the program's requirements and ensure your mail volumes are reported accurately.

**What You’ll Find in This Section**

In the guides below, we walk you through the specifics of the Mail Growth Incentive and other seasonal USPS promotions. We’ve designed these resources to help you:

*   **Timing**: Registration March 1, 2026 through May 31, 2026
    
*   **Understand Qualifications**: Eligible mail volumes: First-Class Letters, Postcards, Flats and Marketing Letters - each Mail Class has its own MGI Program
    
*   **Navigate the USPS Business Customer Gateway**: Step-by-step assistance creating your USPS BCG account
    
*   **MGI Enrollment Process**: Re-enrollment and new program participant registration process
    
*   **Claim Your Postage Credit**: Step-by-step instructions for claiming and assigning your postage credits to Lob
    
*   **USPS Mail Growth Incentive Program Checklist**: [`Download`arrow-up-right](https://s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/public.lob.com/solutions/HelpCenterPromoDocs/2026+USPS+Mail+Growth+Incentive+Program+Registration+Checklist.docx)
     the checklist as a helpful guide
    

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/usps-mail-growth-incentive#registration-period)

Registration Period

The Mail Growth Incentive registration for 2026 opens on March 1, 2026 and runs through May 31, 2026.

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/usps-mail-growth-incentive#mail-growth-incentive-requirements)

Mail Growth Incentive Requirements

*   Mail Owners must register under their own Mail Owners Customer Registration ID (CRID) assigned by USPS.
    
    *   A CRID is a unique, USPS-generated numeric code, up to 15 digits, that identifies a specific business location involved in mailing activities.
        
    *   If a Mail Owner does not have a CRID, one will be assigned during the USPS Business Customer Gateway account creation process.
        
    *   It is incumbent upon the Mail Owner to ensure that the CRID(s) that they mail under are included and approved in their MGI registration and that all mailings that are entered are designated under their registered CRID in order to earn credits.
        
    
*   **Mail Owners must mail a minimum of 1 million mailpieces in either the First-Class Mail or the Marketing Mail classes.**
    
    *   Aggregated volumes for both mail classes are not allowed when determining either the baseline volume or the over-achievement of volume.
        
    
*   **Lob customers with a minimum volume of 800,000 annually AND have the potential to exceed 1,000,000 mailpieces annually** in either First-Class Letters & Postcards or Marketing Letters are encouraged to register for the Mail Growth Incentive program.
    

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/usps-mail-growth-incentive#enrollment-process-for-2026)

Enrollment Process for 2026

#### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/usps-mail-growth-incentive#re-enrollment-for-2025-participants)

Re-Enrollment for 2025 Participants

USPS enhancements to the 2026 Mail Growth Incentive program allows existing customer Mail Owners who participated in the 2025 MGI Program to automatically re-enroll via email.

Expect to receive the emails from the Mail Growth Incentive team beginning March 1, 2026.

💡**If you participated in both First-Class and Marketing Mail Growth Incentive, you will receive separate emails for each. You must confirm the Baseline Volume agreement for each.**

#### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/usps-mail-growth-incentive#confirm-baseline-volume)

**Confirm Baseline Volume**

Mail Owners will need to confirm their baseline volume. The email from USPS will allow you to accept or dispute the reported volume.

*   Baseline volumes are separate for each First-Class Mail and Marketing Mail.
    

Below is an example of the email:

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FXOLt1oJm6OHlK6N1dZvO%252Funknown.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D9e383416-d3c6-4d42-b5b5-c3e1ac08cd46&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=ba03dacd&sv=2)

Upon agreement of baseline volume, the Mail Owner will receive an email confirmation of 2026 MGI program enrollment.

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/usps-mail-growth-incentive#new-mail-growth-incentive-participant-enrollment)

New Mail Growth Incentive Participant Enrollment

New Mail Growth Incentive enrollment is a multi-step process. This section provides instructions for navigating each step successfully.

Step 1: Create a USPS Business Customer Gateway account.

Step 2: Complete the My Products Portal Request Form.

Step 3: Mail Growth Incentive Program Enrollment.

**Step 1: Create a USPS Business Customer Gateway account**

New Mail Growth Incentive enrollment takes place in the USPS Business Customer Gateway portal. If you do not have an existing USPS Business Customer Gateway account, you will need to create an account first.

*   Customers will need to create a USPS Business Customer Gateway account if they do not already have one.
    
*   Refer to [PP-BCG\_SignUp\_1.mp4arrow-up-right](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1NhA52oaFJfbXXzmJXJO8qn9KZfORqX1p/view?usp=drive_link)
     for instructions on how to sign up for a BCG account.
    
*   Once BCG enrollment is complete, this customer will see confirmation on their screen:
    

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FDS9TPVSYqjDStqIgJWY7%252Funknown.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Ddee4cea0-dea3-4e33-9ffb-88dddcc44baa&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=f311fc3d&sv=2)

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/usps-mail-growth-incentive#step-2-complete-the-my-products-portal-request-form)

**Step 2: Complete the My Products Portal Request Form**

The form is located within the My Products Portal.

(The service has been renamed and will be visible as either My Products Portal or MP Portal.)

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FqyrgMYYScJr1uciQAiaE%252Funknown.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Da8f79c30-3790-44fd-bca2-fd457cf5ac17&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=cfad5f3a&sv=2)

**New enrollees will need to complete the portal access request form.**

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FSXtQHdQvZoAfpj4hfNqi%252Funknown.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Db68e990a-cdd5-412a-8d08-a98d37e307db&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=cc09f8c3&sv=2)

**Step 3: Mail Growth Incentive Program Enrollment**

The My Products Portal is segmented into two key sections: Mailing Promotions and Mail Growth Incentives. Select the Mail Growth Incentives section.

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FauXZUIUo51GHHVeafedL%252Funknown.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D00801245-88ed-4f3f-8c85-9ec2d47b665a&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=63a0023d&sv=2)

*   The USPS 20 page resource for step-by-step registration with screenshots are located here:
    
*   [https://postalpro.usps.com/mgi-reg-deckarrow-up-right](https://postalpro.usps.com/mgi-reg-deck)
    
*   [https://postalpro.usps.com/mgi-reg-videoarrow-up-right](https://postalpro.usps.com/mgi-reg-video)
    
*   Full USPS Mailing Promotions Portal User Guide located [herearrow-up-right](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1uMrthQpgRW28p_w4GqLsFD4LW5yrAZMv/view?usp=drive_link)
    .
    

**Confirm and Communicate Enrollment Status**

Users will receive an email when the registration request is approved by USPS and the baseline volume is confirmed by the Program Office.

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FOGhpoahoSSA0odCy95q0%252Funknown.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D84fd6da5-66f3-4d3d-a302-db7b89befc07&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=a54c28f0&sv=2)

Lob recommends sharing their MGI approval confirmation emails and screenshots of confirmed baseline volumes to their Account Manager / Account Executive.

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/usps-mail-growth-incentive#monitor-reported-volume-by-period)

Monitor Reported Volume by Period

💡**It’s important to monitor your reported volumes on a regular basis and alert the MGI team when reported volumes are not aligned with your mail volumes within each period.**

Lob encourages our customers to provide screenshots of the reported actual volume each month to support issue mitigation prior to the Postage Credit Claim reporting period.

**It is the Mail Owner’s responsibility to review volumes and alert the MGI team when Actual Volume is not accurate.**

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FtHIr7TsJ3YJQnvMLzmOB%252Funknown.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D5d332d85-4fed-469d-aa45-186c7cef5dd0&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=b4bf677&sv=2)

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FLewUZ8temSuB9Zh0USWa%252Funknown.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D25efb05d-0fb2-4348-b054-a16f9e5e7c7d&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=de1af847&sv=2)

**If your reported volumes are not aligned with Lob reported volumes during monthly check-ins, you must immediately open an inquiry ticket with the Ask Me team.**

**Refer to the** [**Ask Me**arrow-up-right](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1WtQhcMQ_Sna5DUTX0Z9tV5HCbCxdVbPRbuskvv9sNpw/edit#heading=h.3zyti94olxyh)
 **section for instructions on how to submit a ticket.**

#### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/usps-mail-growth-incentive#requesting-postage-credit-on-a-permit)

Requesting Postage Credit on a Permit

Customers will need to request the Postage Credit through the My Products Portal. The USPS step-by-step instructions are also available for [downloadarrow-up-right](https://postalpro.usps.com/msi/requesting-credit)
 if additional details are needed.

1.  Log into the My Products Portal and select the Mail Growth Incentives button.
    

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FnE6nmXXLTbOixFGuUlum%252Funknown.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D50fb8e26-2b44-4785-a04a-6b8c075555cc&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=8c5f7f48&sv=2)

1.  Select the Incentive Credit Dashboard and then select the SR# that has the Credit information available to create the Permit.
    

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FDDaxhmB9jyuCL6hOfmje%252Funknown.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3De70b8200-1458-433a-813c-72cf17c3db29&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=eee8ecb9&sv=2)

1.  From the Service Request details page, select the Claim Credit tab and button.
    

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FhLJpx7tZQKuTl4tB6m3s%252Funknown.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Dc2998374-6f4c-4f9a-b7d2-9ddae03dce52&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=aabfeeac&sv=2)

1.  Enter the Permit Number and Finance Number in the Permit Search fields, then select the Add Permit button.
    

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FALm4znPXbhiH2sULBJYU%252Funknown.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D6b7a8481-7bdc-4841-b620-678ebc419c4a&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=6542013d&sv=2)

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/usps-mail-growth-incentive#details-to-submit-claim-for-lob-the-mail-service-providers-approval)

Details to submit claim for Lob, the Mail Service Provider’s approval:

**Permit Number: 634**

**Permit Type: PI**

**Finance Number: 056786**

**Lob Email: [\[email protected\]](https://help.lob.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)
**

1.  An acknowledgement message will appear requesting the MSP point of contact email so the Permit request can be sent for their approval.
    
2.  Once the [\[email protected\]envelope](https://help.lob.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#6f3f001c1b0e0342201f0a1d0e1b0600011c2f03000d410c0002)
     email address is entered, select the Confirm button and a confirmation message will populate when the Permit is successfully created and added to the list.
    

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FK5UUsFYUwzMPdnfFnVm3%252Funknown.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Dc2c67830-4783-4043-80d1-ae32aa5cc6c3&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=29496a0e&sv=2)

1.  One the message is acknowledged, the Mail Owner will need to enter the amount of credit needed to be applied to the Permit. Do this by selecting the pencil icon, enter the amount and then select the Save button.
    
2.  If you’ve made a mistake on the amount, just select the Edit button to update the information.
    
3.  Or the X button to remove the Permit from the Credit Claim request and make the change.
    

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252Fr2siPY2yUtcHpKdEyXcZ%252Funknown.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D75e78fca-72e8-4106-8113-ee59cf41edcf&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=374b99bb&sv=2)

1.  A confirmation message will appear stating that the Permit was successfully updated:
    

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FBaue1rugIbiJVgdtyMF0%252Funknown.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D22b3e601-2007-4e3d-ba58-bbb9d18e6101&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=f4dbb419&sv=2)

1.  Once all Permits are updated, select the Submit button and confirm the list of Permits being submitted is accurate.
    

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FRNuNM04GC2JuZlyEdYLE%252Funknown.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D48be6c4c-bcf2-4cb3-b3b8-c01a565a75e9&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=ce43f755&sv=2)

1.  You can view the Postage Credit Claim transfer status in the Incentive Permits pane which is located under the Related tab in the Service Request screen:
    

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FUVnGxLv8vSoKEt1eaIaR%252Funknown.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D93763d2b-040a-4694-9a86-d48a0a3c5617&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=743e0a73&sv=2)

1.  The Permit Status will initially show Submitted for Approval:
    

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FelOV0SXnWAL1b4uXiVdz%252Funknown.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Df8aae1a0-103c-4065-9357-e91cf2ba2188&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=2e300896&sv=2)

1.  Once Lob, the MSP, receives the email, the Partner Operations team will review and Accept the transfer request. You can view the status in the Incentive Permit screen:
    

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252F6izGunxrShn9RZwtjPT7%252Funknown.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D1c427e8b-3c5c-4b74-b2a8-b0879ba81c78&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=753fa064&sv=2)

1.  After Lob accepts the Postage Credit, the Permit Status will change to Pending Program Review:
    

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252F8qUwLgalZhsW5FwI3tmi%252Funknown.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D4d3bdc17-18bf-47d5-96a9-62b592988430&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=c025c298&sv=2)

When Lob customers request their Postage Credit to be transferred to Lob, the Mail Owner will receive an email notification confirming their request. The Program Office will review and approve then Lob will receive an email notification to either accept or reject.

In some cases, Lob has not received the email notification. It is important to keep your Lob representative informed in order for Lob to monitor and support related issues.

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/usps-mail-growth-incentive#update-contact-information-the-my-products-portal)

**Update Contact Information: The My Products Portal**

Below are the instructions to update contact information, if you need to make a change to your organization's main point of contact email address.

*   The USPS will send an email to the contact email address listed in the MP Portal profile.
    
*   Select the dropdown arrow next to the login name and choose My Profile.
    

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FJYhqGp7ZXqE1iQFbQAjg%252Funknown.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Dd83e9dfc-a140-47a2-918b-84f145d47f24&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=9162d704&sv=2)

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FBfTwuE7yh0TJfvph0uAG%252Funknown.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Df7313b09-f947-47be-8b1e-3f2698394ec1&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=e64ac619&sv=2)

*   You can verify or change your email address in your Profile by selecting the Edit button in the top right hand corner.
    

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FXPXDBjW45yPtEciyaUEA%252Funknown.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D0f79fe0f-5c84-4254-949e-898b5376a7e9&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=2be1a8a1&sv=2)

*   If you need to update your email address, select Edit, this launches the Edit User screen.
    
*   Update your email address and select Save.
    

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252F1afK5oeTWLJclBJPzTKa%252Funknown.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D633cc1be-421d-49ea-b857-68ab1fff91a3&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=8e1d6875&sv=2)

**Ask Me: Issue Submission Process**

These are the steps the customers need to take to open a ticket with the MGI team to address the issue:

1.  Log into the BCG
    
2.  Select the MP Portal link:
    

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252F3RisF3CuCViEhvqf5cIJ%252Funknown.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D6df8c710-4d68-49b3-a8b3-2407d82f2444&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=16db95b6&sv=2)

1.  Also listed as My Products Portal (f/k/a My Mailing Promotions):
    

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FR5OkySFToMNZvhN42Wm8%252Funknown.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D32ba907b-5ffc-49f4-9911-9e1460a0e1ff&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=2ef4942f&sv=2)

1.  In the My Products Portal, select the Mail Growth Incentives option
    

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FZqPBxXQKGK7seUrljq4A%252Funknown.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D9e3e62a1-313d-4b47-8410-7c0aa0f191eb&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=7c89eca3&sv=2)

1.  Select the Ask My button located in the middle of the page
    

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FzZJFBvYuDu7WB04oOTTg%252Funknown.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D985b7d59-798e-4a48-8fbe-f9e6929d1682&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=fa859f46&sv=2)

1.  Then select the Mail Growth Incentive Type that they are having an issue on.
    
2.  Complete the Question Summary and the Question area with the details.
    

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252Fbt9K4OgKiM2GNpX7nwru%252Funknown.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D268ad3e3-ca71-4b06-9b27-57a61c6b3151&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=58445521&sv=2)

1.  After selecting Next, you’ll receive confirmation your question has been submitted:
    

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FJY49C1Q1evEiEAienauJ%252Funknown.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D7c8f882b-8e6e-42c8-b870-fb894bb4a840&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=59eb267a&sv=2)

1.  The Mail Owner’s email address on the MPP Profile will receive an email from the resource the USPS assigned to the ticket.
    
2.  Once the Mail Owner receives the email, I encourage them to include their Lob representative and Lori Tiseth to monitor or assist with responding to USPS.

# AV Elements | Lob Help Center

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/address-verification/av-integrations-and-libraries/av-elements#how-to-get-started-0)

How to get started


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/address-verification/av-integrations-and-libraries/av-elements#registration-1)

Registration

Create an account at [Lob.comarrow-up-right](https://www.lob.com/)
 to obtain a **Live Public API Key**. The key is available in the [_API Keys_ tabarrow-up-right](https://dashboard.lob.com/settings/api-keys)
 of the Lob [Dashboard Settingsarrow-up-right](https://dashboard.lob.com/settings/)
 and uses the format, `live_pub_*`

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/address-verification/av-integrations-and-libraries/av-elements#usage-2)

Usage

Address Elements works by targeting the input elements of your address form and using their values with Lob's verification and autocomplete functionality. Include the AV Elements script tag immediately before the closing `<body>` tag

Code demo:

Copy

    <script src="https://cdn.lob.com/lob-address-elements/2.2.1/lob-address-elements.min.merged.js"
      data-lob-key="live_pub_xxx"
    ></script>

Sandbox:

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fstatic.helpjuice.com%2Fhelpjuice_production%2Fuploads%2Fupload%2Fimage%2F9583%2Fdirect%2F1644279576838-1644279576838.png&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=6d01596d&sv=2)

\>> [Click here to open sandbox environmentarrow-up-right](https://codesandbox.io/s/av-elements-plain-js-g91e9?from-embed)

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/address-verification/av-integrations-and-libraries/av-elements#shopify-users-3)

Shopify users

AV elements works great with e-commerce platforms because they use predictable element names. For example, Shopify users can simply paste the following preconfigured script at the bottom of their Shopify Plus template.

circle-check

Remember to replace `live_pub_xxx` with your Lob public key

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fstatic.helpjuice.com%2Fhelpjuice_production%2Fuploads%2Fupload%2Fimage%2F9583%2Fdirect%2F1629292419366-1629292419366.jpeg&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=6f2b556e&sv=2)

circle-exclamation

Many E-commerce platforms have strict content security policies that prevent scripts from loading additional content. Embed the merged build of Address Elements to handle these situations as shown in the example above (`lob-address-elements.min.merged.js`). This ensures all dependencies are included in the download.

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/address-verification/av-integrations-and-libraries/av-elements#attribute-reference-cheatsheet-4)

Attribute reference cheatsheet


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Address Verification script has many attributes to give you complete control over its appearance and behavior. They generally go by the following pattern:

data-lob-\*-id

data-lob-\*-value

These attributes are used to identify an element on your web page. Their values should be the elements ID and correspond to the components of an address: primary line, secondary line, city, state, and zip.

Example: `data-lob-primary-id="address1"`

These attributes modify the behavior of autocomplete and verification. Their values are either true, false, or a string from a list of options

Example: `data-lob-verify-value="passthrough"`

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/address-verification/av-integrations-and-libraries/av-elements#form-detection-attributes-5)

**Form detection attributes**

With version 2, AV Elements can detect address form inputs automatically. We can improve performance by skipping the form detection process when these attributes are provided. Set them to the IDs for each field to target.

*   `data-lob-primary-id`
    
*   `data-lob-secondary-id`
    
*   `data-lob-city-id`
    
*   `data-lob-state-id`
    
*   `data-lob-zip-id`
    
*   `data-lob-country-id`
    

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/address-verification/av-integrations-and-libraries/av-elements#form-functionality-attributes-6)

**Form functionality attributes**

These attributes modify the behavior of AV Elements

Attribute name

Attribute value(s)

`data-lob-verify-value`

*   `strict`
    
*   `normal`
    
*   `relaxed`
    
*   `passthrough`
    
*   `false`
    

Controls the strictness level when verifying an address. If the strictness criteria is not met, we block the form submission and prompt the user to correct the address. Set to `passthrough` to submit the form regardless of the verification result. Set to `false` to disable verification altogether.

Default: `relaxed`

[Learn more about Lob's strictness setting‍](https://help.lob.com/print-and-mail/building-a-mail-strategy/managing-mail-settings#us-mail-strictness-settings-2)

`data-lob-primary-valu`e

Enables or disables address autocompletion.

Default: `true`

`data-lob-secondary-value`

When set to false, force the suite or unit number to render on the primary address line during address verification.

Default: `true`

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/address-verification/av-integrations-and-libraries/av-elements#custom-error-handling-7)

**Custom error handling**

`data-lob-*-message-id` Identifies the element containing the error message at the input level. Lob will update the text content for this element and toggle its display.

*   `data-lob-primary-message-id`
    
*   `data-lob-secondary-message-id`
    
*   `data-lob-city-message-id`
    
*   `data-lob-city-message-id`
    
*   `data-lob-zip-message-id`
    
*   `data-lob-country-message-id`
    

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fstatic.helpjuice.com%2Fhelpjuice_production%2Fuploads%2Fupload%2Fimage%2F9583%2Fdirect%2F1629292739133-1629292739133.png&width=300&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=f913b4f5&sv=2)

Example: `data-lob-primary-message-id` points to the element below the input for Address 1. Any errors related to the primary line will be inserted inside this element.

`data-lob-err-*-value` Overrides the default error message when a value is missing or specific Lob verification error messages

*   `data-lob-err-primary-line-value`
    
*   `data-lob-err-city-state-zip-value`
    
*   `data-lob-err-zip-value`
    
*   `data-lob-err-undeliverable-value`
    
*   `data-lob-err-missing-unit-value`
    
*   `data-lob-err-unnecessary-unit-value`
    
*   `data-lob-err-incorrect-unit-value`
    
*   `data-lob-err-confirm-value`
    
*   `data-lob-err-default-value`
    

`data-lob-anchor-id` This optional attribute will place the general error message before the element with the id provided. An alternative attribute is `data-lob-anchor-class` which will search for the target element by class name.

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/address-verification/av-integrations-and-libraries/av-elements#usage-with-react-vue-8)

Usage with React & Vue


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Modern web frameworks like React and Vue boost developer productivity without sacrificing control. Add IDs to the components of your address inputs (they will be included in the compiled HTML at runtime) then include these IDs in the form detection attributes of the AV elements script.

circle-info

If your component has many layers, you must ensure that the ID is propagated down to the root html components being used.

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/address-verification/av-integrations-and-libraries/av-elements#react-example-9)

React example

React demo

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fstatic.helpjuice.com%2Fhelpjuice_production%2Fuploads%2Fupload%2Fimage%2F9583%2Fdirect%2F1629293249544-1629293249544.png&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=f661fd05&sv=2)

\>> [Click here to open sandbox environmentarrow-up-right](https://codesandbox.io/s/av-elements-react-demo-edxk5?from-embed)

In this demo we use React out of the box and create an address form. The AV elements script is added in `public/index.html` before the closing body tag. This demo also overrides some styles to keep the verification message centered in the form and to undo the styles applied to our primary address by our dependency algolia (for autocompletion).

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/address-verification/av-integrations-and-libraries/av-elements#vue-example-10)

Vue example

Vue 3 demo

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fstatic.helpjuice.com%2Fhelpjuice_production%2Fuploads%2Fupload%2Fimage%2F9583%2Fdirect%2F1629293076910-1629293076910.png&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=28f28f1d&sv=2)

\>> [Click here to open sandbox environmentarrow-up-right](https://codesandbox.io/s/av-elements-vue-demo-gtuvs?from-embed)

Similarly, the AV elements script is also placed in `public/index.html` before the closing body tag.

#### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/address-verification/av-integrations-and-libraries/av-elements#a-note-about-component-libraries-11)

A note about component libraries

The AV elements script searches for key form elements like the form tag and `<input type="submit" />`. When building your form with component libraries such as [Material-UIarrow-up-right](https://material-ui.com/)
 or [Ant Designarrow-up-right](https://ant.design/)
, check for the following:

*   An ID can be propagate down to the root html elements used by the component.
    
*   The components render the appropriate html elements for their behavior - they are materially honest (e.g. not styling links as buttons or using hidden elements to work correctly)
    

Case #1 may be the more common scenario you'll come across. If this happens, you will need to fallback to writing the component yourself in place of using the library's component.

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/address-verification/av-integrations-and-libraries/av-elements#international-support-12)

International support


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

International address verification is enabled when there is a country input present and it is set outside the United States. Form detection is relaxed on international forms since different countries may require different sets of address components.

circle-exclamation

Autocomplete is disabled for international addresses.

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/address-verification/av-integrations-and-libraries/av-elements#troubleshooting-13)

Troubleshooting


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

AV Elements employs multiple strategies to detect your address form. If none of them work, we display an error message to help fix this. Those messages are shown below:

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/address-verification/av-integrations-and-libraries/av-elements#missing-or-duplicate-form-elements-14)

Missing or duplicate form elements

**Problem**: These errors arise in the form detection process:

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fstatic.helpjuice.com%2Fhelpjuice_production%2Fuploads%2Fupload%2Fimage%2F9583%2Fdirect%2F1649181170394-1649181170394.png&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=605736c1&sv=2)

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fstatic.helpjuice.com%2Fhelpjuice_production%2Fuploads%2Fupload%2Fimage%2F9583%2Fdirect%2F1649181188380-1649181188380.png&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=6a940631&sv=2)

Either we are unable to find the input for a given address component, or we detected multiple inputs that may correspond to the same address component. There could be various causes for these errors including the input not existing, a label we're not familiar with, or a problem with the component's implementation from an external library.

**Solution**: Add the attribute(s) mentioned in the error to the AV elements script. The value should be an element id. For example, with the message above we must add the attribute `data-lob-state-id`.

[PreviousAV integrations & librarieschevron-left](https://help.lob.com/address-verification/av-integrations-and-libraries)
[NextShopify App: Address Cleanserchevron-right](https://help.lob.com/address-verification/av-integrations-and-libraries/shopify-app-address-cleanser)

# Visibility of address changes | Lob Help Center

When sending mail or using Address Verification through Lob, addresses can be altered to match USPS requirements. This includes performing actions such as standardization, fixing typos like wrong zip codes, etc. These changes are surfaced back up to you, and if needed you can see the changes made.

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/developer-docs/use-case-guides/visibility-of-address-changes#why-is-this-important-0)

Why is this important?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Some companies may want to update their addresses to what we return when standardizing and correcting—however, some may also want to log what we changed. This could be important for compliance reasons as well as customer experience; showing your end users what was changed could be a very nice touch in your workflow.

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/developer-docs/use-case-guides/visibility-of-address-changes#how-do-we-programmatically-solve-this-problem-1)

How do we solve this problem programmatically?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The way to do this today is to do a string comparison and see what we altered. For example, you will need to compare the address you passed to Lob with the address we returned back. With our AV product, you can retrieve more granular information without having to split out the address data on your own. For example, if you passed in "street" and in the API response you received "ST" in the "Street Suffix" field, you would know that was the specific change. If the "Street Name" was corrected you would see that split out as well. Otherwise, you can compare the full address and pull out the changes from there.

This leaves a lot of room for customization on business logic to determine what is important to your team when classifying something as a change. Oftentimes a change like “Street” to “St '' is not something the business team wants to be surfaced. It’s these types of business rules that are important when handling these alterations.

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fstatic.helpjuice.com%2Fhelpjuice_production%2Fuploads%2Fupload%2Fimage%2F9583%2Fdirect%2F1654105548084-Communication%2520With%2520Lob%2520%2820%29.jpg&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=ea920ddb&sv=2)

Below is an example of creating a postcard and comparing the to address input with the to address response to identify changes.

TypeScript

Python

Ruby

PHP

Java

# Postman & similar tools | Lob Help Center

Postman is a great way for developers to get started on using Lob’s APIs. Our public workspace allows you and your team to collaborate and make sure you have everything you need to get familiar with Lob’s APIs.

*   [Lob’s public workspacearrow-up-right](https://www.postman.com/lobteam/workspace/lob-public-workspace/overview)
    
*   [Collectionsarrow-up-right](https://www.postman.com/lobteam/workspace/lob-public-workspace/documentation/16169677-975ecb9f-ea22-4d8f-a4f9-53a42f2aee03)
    

Watch the video below for an overview or read through the tutorial below.

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/developer-docs/postman-and-similar-tools#id-311b238c-b08e-4ecf-88e1-60ba45f84bed)

**Postman: How to create & test requests, then export source code**


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following will guide you through the process of using Postman to create and test requests for Lob's API and then export the source code in your chosen programming language.

Lob provides an API for automating the sending of postcards, letters, checks, and more. With the help of Postman, we can easily test Lob's API endpoints, understand their behavior, and assist with the development of a custom Lob integration by exporting source code in any modern programming language.

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/developer-docs/postman-and-similar-tools#b4fc482c-566e-46de-8b7f-0961d5768983)

**Requirements**

*   [Download Postman for free from their websitearrow-up-right](https://www.postman.com/)
    
*   Your Lob API key - Can be located under [Account settingsarrow-up-right](https://dashboard.lob.com/settings/api-keys)
     in your Lob dashboard
    

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/developer-docs/postman-and-similar-tools#id-83333c7d-58bf-4faa-b7ec-eead06832876)

Step 1: Launch Postman

Once you’ve downloaded Postman, follow the installation instructions and launch the app once it’s ready.

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/developer-docs/postman-and-similar-tools#id-74cfe26b-7885-4516-87cc-338ca40eedd0)

Step 2: Create a request

1.  Click on the "New" button located in the top left corner of the application.
    
2.  From the modal that appears, select "HTTP".
    

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252Fd3hR6GXmq92XD3HrlbWd%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Dcc8945a0-4666-497a-9aae-9cfa3e0f2865&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=e9b2727d&sv=2)

1.  Once you’ve configured your request using the instructions below, you can used the “Save” button to select a name and save the request to a collection.
    

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/developer-docs/postman-and-similar-tools#d2f8ab3e-866e-48dd-9ea5-fd30fd968cfb)

Step 3: Configure your request

1.  You will see a new tab open in Postman.
    
2.  From the dropdown menu on the left of the URL bar, select the required HTTP method (GET, POST, DELETE, etc.).
    

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FgTFWblVZPzgN9FioItWb%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Dcedbbfac-be41-4fa7-b03f-11460ebcb626&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=57c583b1&sv=2)

1.  Input the desired Lob API endpoint into the URL field (e.g., `**https://api.lob.com/v1/postcards**`).
    
2.  To authenticate, click on the **Authorization** tab below the **URL** field. Choose “Basic Auth” \*\*\*\*from the **Type** dropdown menu. In the **Username** field, enter your Lob API key, leave the **Password** field blank.
    

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FNp48jFnKWh3NKLv6FPJJ%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D96bbf2c4-93c6-4058-a4b9-fb5d1de4d3e1&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=b285ab46&sv=2)

1.  Depending on the request type, you may need to include additional information. For example, for POST requests, navigate to the "Body" tab, select "raw" and choose "JSON" from the dropdown menu (defaulted to “Text”). Here, you can input the data for your request in JSON format.
    

circle-info

For fields that are intended to accept only string values, submitting JSON objects in string format (stringified JSON objects) is not supported. Our system automatically parses and validates incoming data according to their expected types. As a result, providing a stringified JSON object in a field designated for string input can cause parsing errors or lead to unexpected validation results.

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FtRunLuCYaXCYhT6ejQ20%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Dd5b3fd0b-3492-4307-9fb4-44cb72603ba9&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=766cced&sv=2)

1.  If you’d like to send a file in your API request (for example, a PDF file), you can do so by following the instructions below. If you do not need to send a file (e.g. if you are not sending a POST request or you are using HTML templates), you can skip this step.
    

a. Select the “Body” tab and toggle into “form-data”.

b. From here, you should see a table with columns labelled “Key”, “Value”, and “Description”. In the first empty row, hover on the “Key” field cell to display a dropdown selector with the default datatype option “Text” selected. Click on this dropdown and select “File”.

c. You should se a button labelled “Select Files” appear in the “Value” field cell. Click this button to open the file browser and select the file you’d like to send.

d. Ensure the “Key” cell contains the value “file” (if creating a letter) or “front”/”back” (If creating a postcard or self-mailer).

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FJXqOS7XsmxfIrgOkXolO%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D61471570-3302-4e60-8b45-56c75dcc32c4&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=a9797ceb&sv=2)

![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252FjTDpGJVG8LUdz2psAFSA%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D005770ac-818d-4910-ba7a-37033d5d9bd3&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=7e29e2cd&sv=2)

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/developer-docs/postman-and-similar-tools#id-83441eac-90e1-48c5-aacb-fc46c18be1e9)

Step 4: Send your request and test the response

1.  Click on the blue "Send" button to the right of the URL bar.
    
2.  The response will be displayed in the section below the request fields.
    
3.  You can review the status, time, size, and headers of the response.
    

### 

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/developer-docs/postman-and-similar-tools#d4b05e25-1903-4574-970f-855c83f2f8bf)

Step 5: Export the source code

1.  Click on the "Code" button located on the far right side (it should look like an empty HTML tag, i.e., ”</>”).
    
    ![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252F1vW2lp8TImRnnfnHd1PP%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D637f92dc-5462-433b-b607-ac125fbdb701&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=1ca2b881&sv=2)
    
2.  In the **Code snippets** panel that appears, select your preferred language from the dropdown on the left side.
    
    ![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F1775164782-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FiXDYGxBp6aAwhpEhTMNC%252Fuploads%252Flkf98DTsBnol8klUzZnd%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3De20e9f79-5e77-4568-b389-7bf0c1fe9905&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=227b741b&sv=2)
    
3.  The right side will display the request code in the selected language.
    
4.  Click "Copy snippet" or manually select and copy the code.
    

**That’s all you need to test requests for Lob's API, and export the source code in your chosen programming language!**

circle-check

Postman can be an invaluable part of your API development and testing workflows. Always refer to the [Lob API documentationarrow-up-right](https://docs.lob.com/)
 for any API-specific information and requirements.

[hashtag](https://help.lob.com/developer-docs/postman-and-similar-tools#other-tools)

Other tools


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

curl

insomnia

OpenAPI

[![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fstatic.helpjuice.com%2Fhelpjuice_production%2Fuploads%2Fupload%2Fimage%2F9583%2Fdirect%2F1647464112970-http-domain-svgrepo-com.svg&width=40&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=73561e44&sv=2)arrow-up-right](https://curl.se/)
 [curl.searrow-up-right](https://curl.se/)

**Usage**

cURL is used in command lines or scripts to transfer data. We've included cURL code snippets in our documentation for each resource. By including your API key, you can run these commands from the terminal and see the response from Lob's API.

Explore Lob's [API documentationarrow-up-right](https://docs.lob.com/)
 for more cURL examples.

[![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fstatic.helpjuice.com%2Fhelpjuice_production%2Fuploads%2Fupload%2Fimage%2F9583%2Fdirect%2F1647465234443-insomnia-svgrepo-com%2520%281%29.svg&width=300&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=9d739fe3&sv=2)arrow-up-right](https://github.com/lob/lob-insomnia)
 [lob-insomniaarrow-up-right](https://github.com/lob/lob-insomnia)

**Usage**

Insomnia REST client is a free cross-platform desktop framework for testing RESTful applications. We've created a collection for Lob's API endpoint for use with Insomnia.

[![Run in Insomnia}](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcamo.githubusercontent.com%2Faea93669640167416980164768783f94e76ed3dd4b0fb6b96eacaa7063023176%2F68747470733a2f2f696e736f6d6e69612e726573742f696d616765732f72756e2e737667&width=300&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=440abd5a&sv=2)arrow-up-right](https://insomnia.rest/run/?label=Lob%20Insomnia&uri=https%3A%2F%2Fraw.githubusercontent.com%2Flob%2Flob-insomnia%2Fmain%2Flob-insomnia-1.0.0.json)

Learn more in [lob-insomniaarrow-up-right](https://www.github.com/lob/lob-insomnia)
 repository on GitHub.

[![](https://help.lob.com/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fstatic.helpjuice.com%2Fhelpjuice_production%2Fuploads%2Fupload%2Fimage%2F9583%2Fdirect%2F1647464731759-openapiinitiative-svgrepo-com.svg&width=300&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=c0593da1&sv=2)arrow-up-right](https://github.com/lob/lob-openapi)
 [lob-openapiarrow-up-right](https://github.com/lob/lob-openapi)

**Usage**

We've written an OpenAPI v3 authored specification for the current Lob API. This provides users the ability to generate SDKs in languages not currently supported by Lob, import Lob operations and schemas into popular tools that support OpenAPI or define operations and schemas in middleware solutions.

Learn more at the [lob-openapiarrow-up-right](https://www.github.com/lob/lob-openapi)
 repository on GitHub.
